@page "/account/passkeys"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize]
@using System.Buffers.Text
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Authentication
@inject UserManager<Person> UserManager
@inject SignInManager<Person> SignInManager
@inject ISecurityEventLog SecurityEventLog
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* The voluntary passkey-management page (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.3): any signed-in user can add a
   passkey, rename it, or remove it. Static SSR, like every account page — registering a passkey writes
   through the Identity store and records a security_event, and the ceremony's challenge lives in a
   cookie the server sets. Not in the obligations-exempt list: it is a normal authenticated destination,
   so a user with an outstanding obligation is routed to the relevant pipeline page, never here.

   Forms on this page all submit natively (the add button via the passkey-submit custom element, the
   per-row buttons as ordinary posts), each carrying its @formname so the framework can dispatch it:
     • "add-passkey"      — the attestation credential the browser produced (bound to Input);
     • "passkey-{id}"     — one per credential, carrying the credential id, an Action (rename|delete),
                            and the new name for a rename. The shared Action/CredentialId/NewName bind
                            from whichever row posted. *@

<PageTitle>Passkeys</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">Security</p>
    <h1>Passkeys</h1>
    <p class="lede">
        A passkey lets you sign in with your fingerprint, face, or device PIN instead of a password —
        and it can't be phished. Add one for each device you use. You can keep your password as a
        backup.
    </p>

    @if (_statusMessage is not null)
    {
        <p class="status-success" role="status">@_statusMessage</p>
    }
    @if (_errorMessage is not null)
    {
        <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
    }

    @if (_passkeys is { Count: > 0 })
    {
        <ul class="passkey-list">
            @foreach (UserPasskeyInfo passkey in _passkeys)
            {
                string credentialId = Base64Url.EncodeToString(passkey.CredentialId);
                <li class="passkey-row">
                    <div class="passkey-meta">
                        <span class="passkey-name">@(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(passkey.Name) ? "Unnamed passkey" : passkey.Name)</span>
                        <span class="passkey-date">Added @passkey.CreatedAt.ToLocalTime().ToString("d MMM yyyy")</span>
                    </div>
                    <form class="passkey-actions" @formname="@($"passkey-{credentialId}")" method="post" @onsubmit="UpdatePasskeyAsync">
                        <AntiforgeryToken />
                        <input type="hidden" name="CredentialId" value="@credentialId" />
                        <input class="passkey-rename" name="NewName" value="@passkey.Name"
                               aria-label="Passkey name" maxlength="64" />
                        <button type="submit" name="Action" value="rename" class="button-secondary">Rename</button>
                        <button type="submit" name="Action" value="delete" class="link-button danger">Remove</button>
                    </form>
                </li>
            }
        </ul>
    }
    else
    {
        <p class="lede">You have no passkeys yet. Add one so you can sign in without a password.</p>
    }

    <form class="passkey-add" @formname="add-passkey" method="post" @onsubmit="AddPasskeyAsync">
        <AntiforgeryToken />
        <PasskeySubmit Operation="PasskeyOperation.Create" Name="Input" class="button-primary">
            Add a passkey
        </PasskeySubmit>
    </form>
</section>

@code {
    private IList<UserPasskeyInfo>? _passkeys;
    private string? _statusMessage;
    private string? _errorMessage;

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm(FormName = "add-passkey")]
    private PasskeyInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    // Shared by every per-row "passkey-{id}" form; only the submitted row's values arrive.
    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private string? Action { get; set; }

    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private string? CredentialId { get; set; }

    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private string? NewName { get; set; }

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery(Name = "status")]
    private string? Status { get; set; }

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // BL0008: a form POST binds Input itself and may assign null, so the default lives here.
        Input ??= new();
        _statusMessage = Status;

        Person? user = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (user is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        _passkeys = await UserManager.GetPasskeysAsync(user);
    }

    private async Task AddPasskeyAsync()
    {
        Person? user = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (user is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(Input.Error))
        {
            await FailAsync(user, "That passkey could not be added. Please try again.");
            return;
        }

        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(Input.CredentialJson))
        {
            await FailAsync(user, "Your browser did not provide a passkey.");
            return;
        }

        PasskeyAttestationResult attestation = await SignInManager.PerformPasskeyAttestationAsync(Input.CredentialJson);
        if (!attestation.Succeeded || attestation.Passkey is null)
        {
            await FailAsync(user, "That passkey could not be registered. Please try again.");
            return;
        }

        // The authenticator does not supply a name; give it a readable default the user can change.
        UserPasskeyInfo passkey = attestation.Passkey;
        passkey.Name = $"Passkey added {DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToLocalTime():d MMM yyyy}";

        IdentityResult added = await UserManager.AddOrUpdatePasskeyAsync(user, passkey);
        if (!added.Succeeded)
        {
            await FailAsync(user, "The passkey could not be saved to your account.");
            return;
        }

        await SecurityEventLog.RecordAsync(
            user.PersonIdentifier, actorPersonIdentifier: null, SecurityEventType.PasskeyRegistered, HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        Redirect("Passkey added. You can rename it below.");
    }

    private async Task UpdatePasskeyAsync()
    {
        Person? user = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (user is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        byte[] credentialId;
        try
        {
            credentialId = Base64Url.DecodeFromChars(CredentialId);
        }
        catch (FormatException)
        {
            await FailAsync(user, "That passkey could not be identified.");
            return;
        }

        if (string.Equals(Action, "delete", StringComparison.Ordinal))
        {
            IdentityResult removed = await UserManager.RemovePasskeyAsync(user, credentialId);
            if (!removed.Succeeded)
            {
                await FailAsync(user, "The passkey could not be removed.");
                return;
            }

            await SecurityEventLog.RecordAsync(
                user.PersonIdentifier, actorPersonIdentifier: null, SecurityEventType.PasskeyRemoved, HttpContext.RequestAborted);
            Redirect("Passkey removed.");
            return;
        }

        if (string.Equals(Action, "rename", StringComparison.Ordinal))
        {
            UserPasskeyInfo? existing = await UserManager.GetPasskeyAsync(user, credentialId);
            if (existing is null)
            {
                await FailAsync(user, "That passkey no longer exists.");
                return;
            }

            // Blank keeps the current name; the store's update path writes only the mutable fields,
            // so the public key and backup-eligible bit captured at registration are untouched.
            existing.Name = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(NewName) ? existing.Name : NewName.Trim();
            IdentityResult renamed = await UserManager.AddOrUpdatePasskeyAsync(user, existing);
            if (!renamed.Succeeded)
            {
                await FailAsync(user, "The passkey could not be renamed.");
                return;
            }

            Redirect("Passkey renamed.");
            return;
        }

        await FailAsync(user, "That action was not recognized.");
    }

    // Re-load the list and show an error on the same render (no redirect, so no status query needed).
    private async Task FailAsync(Person user, string message)
    {
        _errorMessage = message;
        _passkeys = await UserManager.GetPasskeysAsync(user);
    }

    // Post/redirect/get: carry a one-line confirmation back through the query string.
    private void Redirect(string status)
        => Navigation.NavigateTo($"{AccountRoutes.Passkeys}?status={Uri.EscapeDataString(status)}");
}

--- CONTENT ---
@page "/sign-in"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@inject SignInManager<Person> SignInManager
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* The sign-in page (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.3, §3.5, §4.1). Static SSR on purpose: issuing the
   authentication cookie needs a real HTTP response, which a Blazor circuit does not have. The form
   is a plain full-page POST (EditForm in SSR emits the antiforgery token itself). Auditing and
   metering happen centrally in RestaurantSignInManager, never here.

   Password and passkey share one form. It uses OnSubmit (not OnValidSubmit) so the passkey button can
   skip the password DataAnnotations: the passkey-submit element writes the assertion credential into
   Input.Passkey and submits natively, and the handler runs PasskeySignInAsync on that before it
   validates anything. A passkey sign-in never gets a TOTP challenge (§3.5) — that is guaranteed in the
   sign-in manager, not here. The username carries autocomplete="username webauthn" so browsers can
   surface a discoverable passkey inline (conditional mediation). *@

<PageTitle>Sign in</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">MyRestaurant</p>
    <h1>Sign in</h1>

    @if (HttpContext.User.Identity?.IsAuthenticated == true)
    {
        <p class="lede">
            You are already signed in as <strong>@HttpContext.User.Identity?.Name</strong>.
        </p>
        <p><a href="/">Back to the home page</a></p>
    }
    else
    {
        @if (_errorMessage is not null)
        {
            <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
        }

        <EditForm EditContext="_editContext" method="post" OnSubmit="SignInUserAsync" FormName="sign-in">
            <DataAnnotationsValidator />

            <div class="form-field">
                <label for="username">Username</label>
                <InputText id="username" @bind-Value="Input.Username" autocomplete="username webauthn"
                           autocapitalize="none" spellcheck="false" autofocus />
                <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.Username)" />
            </div>

            <div class="form-field">
                <label for="password">Password</label>
                <InputText id="password" type="password" @bind-Value="Input.Password"
                           autocomplete="current-password" />
                <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.Password)" />
            </div>

            <div class="form-actions">
                <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Sign in</button>
            </div>

            <div class="sign-in-alternative">
                <span class="sign-in-or">or</span>
                <PasskeySubmit Operation="PasskeyOperation.Request" Name="Input.Passkey"
                               EmailName="Input.Username" class="button-secondary">
                    Sign in with a passkey
                </PasskeySubmit>
            </div>
        </EditForm>

        <p class="form-footnote">
            Guests register at the table by scanning its display — there is no stand-alone
            registration page. Lost access? Ask at the counter.
        </p>
    }
</section>

@code {
    private string? _errorMessage;
    private EditContext _editContext = default!;

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private SignInInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? ReturnUrl { get; set; }

    // BL0008: on a static-SSR form POST the framework binds [SupplyParameterFromForm] itself and may
    // assign null, silently discarding a `= new()` initializer — so the default is applied here instead.
    // OnSubmit means we validate by hand (below), so we drive an explicit EditContext.
    protected override void OnInitialized()
    {
        Input ??= new();
        _editContext = new EditContext(Input);
    }

    private async Task SignInUserAsync()
    {
        string destination = ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl);

        // Passkey path (§3.3): the element posted an assertion credential (or an error). Handle it
        // before any password validation — the username/password fields are irrelevant here — and
        // note PasskeySignInAsync never returns RequiresTwoFactor (a passkey bypasses the §3.5 second
        // factor by construction).
        if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(Input.Passkey?.Error))
        {
            _errorMessage = "That passkey could not be used. Try again, or sign in with your password.";
            return;
        }

        if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(Input.Passkey?.CredentialJson))
        {
            SignInResult passkeyResult = await SignInManager.PasskeySignInAsync(Input.Passkey.CredentialJson);

            if (passkeyResult.Succeeded)
            {
                Navigation.NavigateTo(destination);
            }
            else if (passkeyResult.IsLockedOut)
            {
                _errorMessage = "Too many failed attempts. The account is locked for about five minutes — try again then.";
            }
            else if (passkeyResult.IsNotAllowed)
            {
                _errorMessage = "This account has been deactivated. Ask at the counter if you think that is a mistake.";
            }
            else
            {
                _errorMessage = "That passkey did not work. Try again, or sign in with your password.";
            }

            return;
        }

        // Password path: OnSubmit fires regardless of validity, so enforce the DataAnnotations now.
        if (!_editContext.Validate())
        {
            return;
        }

        // isPersistent: the §3.1 session is a 24-hour sliding cookie either way; persisting it lets
        // a guest's phone survive a browser restart mid-meal. lockoutOnFailure per §3.1.
        SignInResult result = await SignInManager.PasswordSignInAsync(
            Input.Username,
            Input.Password,
            isPersistent: true,
            lockoutOnFailure: true);

        if (result.Succeeded)
        {
            // The obligations middleware intercepts this navigation when a §3.5 flag is set.
            Navigation.NavigateTo(destination);
        }
        else if (result.RequiresTwoFactor)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(
                $"{AccountRoutes.SignInTwoFactor}?ReturnUrl={Uri.EscapeDataString(destination)}");
        }
        else if (result.IsLockedOut)
        {
            _errorMessage = "Too many failed attempts. The account is locked for about five minutes — try again then.";
        }
        else if (result.IsNotAllowed)
        {
            _errorMessage = "This account has been deactivated. Ask at the counter if you think that is a mistake.";
        }
        else
        {
            _errorMessage = "That username and password combination did not work.";
        }
    }

    private sealed class SignInInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter your username.")]
        public string Username { get; set; } = string.Empty;

        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter your password.")]
        public string Password { get; set; } = string.Empty;

        // Populated by the passkey-submit element on the assertion path; ignored by the password path.
        // Not [Required] — the passkey button must submit without a username or password.
        public PasskeyInputModel? Passkey { get; set; }
    }
}

@page "/sign-in/recovery-code"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@inject SignInManager<Person> SignInManager
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* A recovery code standing in for the TOTP code on the password path (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.4).
   Each code is single-use; a successful redemption is recorded (`recovery_code_used`) by
   RestaurantSignInManager. Like the TOTP page, this is only reachable mid-sign-in. *@

<PageTitle>Recovery code</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">MyRestaurant</p>
    <h1>Use a recovery code</h1>
    <p class="lede">
        Enter one of the recovery codes you saved when you enrolled your authenticator. Each code
        works exactly once — when you are back in, consider regenerating a fresh set from your
        profile.
    </p>

    @if (_errorMessage is not null)
    {
        <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
    }

    <EditForm Model="Input" method="post" OnValidSubmit="RedeemCodeAsync" FormName="sign-in-recovery-code">
        <DataAnnotationsValidator />

        <div class="form-field">
            <label for="recovery-code">Recovery code</label>
            <InputText id="recovery-code" @bind-Value="Input.RecoveryCode" autocomplete="off"
                       autocapitalize="none" spellcheck="false" autofocus />
            <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.RecoveryCode)" />
        </div>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Sign in</button>
        </div>
    </EditForm>

    <p class="form-footnote">
        Found your authenticator after all?
        <a href="@TwoFactorHref">Enter the six-digit code instead.</a>
    </p>
</section>

@code {
    private string? _errorMessage;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private RecoveryCodeInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? ReturnUrl { get; set; }

    private string TwoFactorHref
        => $"{AccountRoutes.SignInTwoFactor}?ReturnUrl={Uri.EscapeDataString(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl))}";

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // BL0008: a form POST binds Input itself and may assign null, so the default lives here
        // rather than in a property initializer the binder would silently discard.
        Input ??= new();

        if (await SignInManager.GetTwoFactorAuthenticationUserAsync() is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
        }
    }

    private async Task RedeemCodeAsync()
    {
        if (await SignInManager.GetTwoFactorAuthenticationUserAsync() is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        SignInResult result = await SignInManager.TwoFactorRecoveryCodeSignInAsync(Input.RecoveryCode.Trim());

        if (result.Succeeded)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl));
        }
        else if (result.IsLockedOut)
        {
            _errorMessage = "Too many failed attempts. The account is locked for about five minutes — try again then.";
        }
        else
        {
            _errorMessage = "That recovery code did not work. Codes are single-use — try another from your saved set.";
        }
    }

    private sealed class RecoveryCodeInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter a recovery code.")]
        public string RecoveryCode { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}

@page "/sign-in/two-factor"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@inject SignInManager<Person> SignInManager
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* The TOTP challenge on the password path (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.4, §4.2, ADR-0010). Reachable
   only mid-sign-in: the password step parked the pending user in the two-factor cookie; without it
   this page bounces back to /sign-in. There is deliberately no "remember this device" — TOTP is
   challenged on every password sign-in of an enrolled account. The passkey path never lands here. *@

<PageTitle>Two-factor code</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">MyRestaurant</p>
    <h1>Enter your authenticator code</h1>
    <p class="lede">
        Your password was correct. Open your authenticator app and enter the six-digit code for this
        account.
    </p>

    @if (_errorMessage is not null)
    {
        <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
    }

    <EditForm Model="Input" method="post" OnValidSubmit="VerifyCodeAsync" FormName="sign-in-two-factor">
        <DataAnnotationsValidator />

        <div class="form-field">
            <label for="totp-code">Authenticator code</label>
            <InputText id="totp-code" @bind-Value="Input.Code" autocomplete="one-time-code"
                       inputmode="numeric" autofocus />
            <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.Code)" />
        </div>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Verify</button>
        </div>
    </EditForm>

    <p class="form-footnote">
        Authenticator unavailable?
        <a href="@RecoveryCodeHref">Use a recovery code instead.</a>
    </p>
</section>

@code {
    private string? _errorMessage;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private TwoFactorInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? ReturnUrl { get; set; }

    private string RecoveryCodeHref
        => $"{AccountRoutes.SignInRecoveryCode}?ReturnUrl={Uri.EscapeDataString(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl))}";

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // BL0008: a form POST binds Input itself and may assign null, so the default lives here
        // rather than in a property initializer the binder would silently discard.
        Input ??= new();

        // No pending two-factor user means this page was reached out of order — start over.
        if (await SignInManager.GetTwoFactorAuthenticationUserAsync() is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
        }
    }

    private async Task VerifyCodeAsync()
    {
        if (await SignInManager.GetTwoFactorAuthenticationUserAsync() is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        // Authenticator apps display codes in groups; be forgiving about spaces and dashes.
        string code = Input.Code.Replace(" ", string.Empty).Replace("-", string.Empty);

        SignInResult result = await SignInManager.TwoFactorAuthenticatorSignInAsync(
            code,
            isPersistent: true,
            rememberClient: false);

        if (result.Succeeded)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl));
        }
        else if (result.IsLockedOut)
        {
            _errorMessage = "Too many failed attempts. The account is locked for about five minutes — try again then.";
        }
        else
        {
            _errorMessage = "That code did not verify. Codes rotate every 30 seconds — check the app and try the current one.";
        }
    }

    private sealed class TwoFactorInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter the six-digit code.")]
        public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}

@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Antiforgery
@using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection
@inject IServiceProvider Services

@*
    Renders the passkey button plus the form-associated <passkey-submit> custom element (passkey.js).
    The element needs an antiforgery token it can send as a request header on its fetch() to the
    ceremony endpoints (those endpoints are not form posts, so the token cannot ride along in form
    data); we resolve it here from IAntiforgery and hand it over via attributes. Adapted from the
    ASP.NET Core Blazor Web template's PasskeySubmit component.
*@

<button type="submit" name="__passkeySubmit" @attributes="AdditionalAttributes">@ChildContent</button>
<passkey-submit
    operation="@Operation"
    name="@Name"
    email-name="@EmailName"
    creation-options-url="@CreationOptionsUrl"
    request-options-url="@RequestOptionsUrl"
    request-token-name="@_tokens?.HeaderName"
    request-token-value="@_tokens?.RequestToken">
</passkey-submit>

@code {
    private AntiforgeryTokenSet? _tokens;

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    /// <summary>Which ceremony to drive — <see cref="PasskeyOperation.Create"/> or <see cref="PasskeyOperation.Request"/>.</summary>
    [Parameter]
    [EditorRequired]
    public PasskeyOperation Operation { get; set; }

    /// <summary>Form-field prefix the element writes back to (e.g. "Input" or "Input.Passkey").</summary>
    [Parameter]
    [EditorRequired]
    public string Name { get; set; } = default!;

    /// <summary>For the Request operation: the username field to read for the username-first flow.</summary>
    [Parameter]
    public string? EmailName { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Overrides the creation (attestation) options endpoint the element fetches. Null (the default)
    /// leaves passkey.js on <see cref="AccountRoutes.PasskeyCreationOptions"/>; the setup wizard sets
    /// it to <see cref="AccountRoutes.SetupPasskeyCreationOptions"/> (§3.6).
    /// </summary>
    [Parameter]
    public string? CreationOptionsUrl { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Overrides the request (assertion) options endpoint the element fetches. Null (the default)
    /// leaves passkey.js on <see cref="AccountRoutes.PasskeyRequestOptions"/>. Provided for symmetry;
    /// the setup wizard only registers passkeys, so it does not set this.
    /// </summary>
    [Parameter]
    public string? RequestOptionsUrl { get; set; }

    [Parameter]
    public RenderFragment? ChildContent { get; set; }

    [Parameter(CaptureUnmatchedValues = true)]
    public IDictionary<string, object>? AdditionalAttributes { get; set; }

    protected override void OnInitialized()
        => _tokens = Services.GetService<IAntiforgery>()?.GetTokens(HttpContext);
}

@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* Sends an anonymous visitor to the sign-in page, preserving where they were headed. forceLoad is
   required: the sign-in page is excluded from interactive routing (static SSR), so the interactive
   router cannot navigate to it client-side — a full document load is the only correct transition.
   During static prerendering the NavigateTo call surfaces as an HTTP redirect instead. *@

@code {
    protected override void OnInitialized()
    {
        string destination = "/" + Navigation.ToBaseRelativePath(Navigation.Uri);
        Navigation.NavigateTo(
            $"{AccountRoutes.SignIn}?ReturnUrl={Uri.EscapeDataString(destination)}",
            forceLoad: true);
    }
}

################################################################################
# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/App.razor

@* The root HTML document. Interactive-server rendering is the default for the whole app (ADR-0004),
   but it is applied per page rather than hard-coded: pages marked [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
   (the account/sign-in surfaces) render as static SSR instead, because writing the authentication
   cookie requires a real HTTP response — a Blazor circuit cannot set cookies (BUILD_PROGRESS:
   "sign-in pages must post to a static/SSR endpoint"). *@
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
    <base href="/" />
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="app.css" />
    <link rel="icon" href="data:," />
    @* Defines the <passkey-submit> custom element (§3.3). A plain classic script, loaded up front so
       the element is registered for the static-SSR account pages, which run no Blazor circuit. *@
    <script src="js/passkey.js" defer></script>
    <HeadOutlet @rendermode="RenderModeForPage" />
</head>

<body>
    <Routes @rendermode="RenderModeForPage" />
    <script src="_framework/blazor.web.js"></script>
</body>

</html>

@code {
    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    /// <summary>
    /// Interactive server for ordinary pages; <c>null</c> (static SSR) for pages that opted out with
    /// <see cref="ExcludeFromInteractiveRoutingAttribute"/> — the sign-in and obligation pages.
    /// </summary>
    private IComponentRenderMode? RenderModeForPage
        => HttpContext.AcceptsInteractiveRouting() ? InteractiveServer : null;
}

@inherits LayoutComponentBase

@* The shared shell. The session header uses a plain HTML form for sign-out — a full-page POST to
   the /sign-out endpoint with the antiforgery token — so it behaves identically whether the hosting
   page renders as static SSR (the account pages) or over an interactive circuit. The Administration
   link is shown only to the administrator role: <AuthorizeView Roles="administrator"> matches the role
   claim the RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory emits under the configured RoleClaimType, the same claim
   the area.administration policy checks (§3.7). *@
<div class="app-shell">
    <header class="app-header">
        <a class="app-brand" href="/">MyRestaurant</a>
        <nav class="app-session" aria-label="Session">
            <AuthorizeView>
                <Authorized>
                    <span class="session-name">@context.User.Identity?.Name</span>
                    <AuthorizeView Roles="administrator" Context="administrator">
                        <a class="session-link" href="/administration">Administration</a>
                    </AuthorizeView>
                    <a class="session-link" href="@AccountRoutes.TotpEnrollment">Security</a>
                    <a class="session-link" href="@AccountRoutes.Passkeys">Passkeys</a>
                    <form class="sign-out-form" method="post" action="@AccountRoutes.SignOut">
                        <AntiforgeryToken />
                        <button type="submit" class="link-button">Sign out</button>
                    </form>
                </Authorized>
                <NotAuthorized>
                    <a class="session-link" href="@AccountRoutes.SignIn">Sign in</a>
                </NotAuthorized>
            </AuthorizeView>
        </nav>
    </header>

    <main class="app-main">
        @Body
    </main>
</div>

@page "/administration"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize(Policy = AuthorizationPolicies.Administration)]
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Time
@inject IPersonDirectory People
@inject IClock Clock

@* The administration landing (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.6/§3.7 — `area.administration`: the
   administrator role). It lists every account with its roles and status, links each to its management
   page, and offers staff creation. Static SSR, like every account/administration surface, so the
   management pages it links can post full pages and write the redirect/cookie responses a Blazor
   circuit cannot. Endpoint authorization enforces the policy on the server for this static-SSR page (an
   anonymous visitor is sent to /sign-in, a signed-in non-administrator to /access-denied). Not exempt
   from the obligations pipeline: an administrator with an outstanding §3.5 flag is routed to clear it
   first. *@

<PageTitle>Administration — people</PageTitle>

<section class="panel">
    <div class="admin-header">
        <div>
            <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
            <h1>People</h1>
        </div>
        <div class="admin-header-actions">
            <a class="button-secondary" href="/administration/tables">Tables</a>
            <a class="button-primary" href="/administration/people/new">Create staff account</a>
        </div>
    </div>

    <p class="lede">
        Every account in the system, oldest first. Guests appear here once they register by joining a
        table; staff are created here. Open a person to grant or revoke roles, reset their credentials,
        or deactivate the account.
    </p>

    @if (_people is null)
    {
        <p class="lede">Loading…</p>
    }
    else if (_people.Count == 0)
    {
        <p class="lede">No accounts yet.</p>
    }
    else
    {
        <div class="admin-people">
            <table>
                <thead>
                    <tr>
                        <th scope="col">Username</th>
                        <th scope="col">Roles</th>
                        <th scope="col">Credentials</th>
                        <th scope="col">Status</th>
                        <th scope="col"><span class="visually-hidden">Actions</span></th>
                    </tr>
                </thead>
                <tbody>
                    @foreach (PersonSummary person in _people)
                    {
                        <tr>
                            <td>
                                <span class="person-username">@person.Username</span>
                                @if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(person.DisplayName))
                                {
                                    <span class="person-display-name">@person.DisplayName</span>
                                }
                            </td>
                            <td>
                                @if (person.Roles.Count == 0)
                                {
                                    <span class="muted">—</span>
                                }
                                else
                                {
                                    @foreach (string role in person.Roles)
                                    {
                                        <span class="chip chip-role">@role</span>
                                    }
                                }
                            </td>
                            <td>
                                @if (person.HasPassword)
                                {
                                    <span class="chip">Password</span>
                                }
                                @if (person.HasAuthenticator)
                                {
                                    <span class="chip">Authenticator</span>
                                }
                                @if (!person.HasPassword && !person.HasAuthenticator)
                                {
                                    <span class="muted">—</span>
                                }
                            </td>
                            <td>
                                @if (!person.IsActive)
                                {
                                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Deactivated</span>
                                }
                                else if (IsLockedOut(person))
                                {
                                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Locked until @(person.LockoutEndAt!.Value.ToLocalTime().ToString("g"))</span>
                                }
                                else
                                {
                                    <span class="chip chip-ok">Active</span>
                                }

                                @if (person.MustChangePassword)
                                {
                                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Must change password</span>
                                }
                                @if (person.MustEnrollTotp)
                                {
                                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Must set up authenticator</span>
                                }
                            </td>
                            <td class="admin-row-actions">
                                <a href="@($"/administration/people/{person.PersonIdentifier}")">Manage</a>
                            </td>
                        </tr>
                    }
                </tbody>
            </table>
        </div>
    }

    <p class="form-footnote">
        Role and credential changes take effect on affected sessions within a few minutes (the
        security-stamp revalidation window, §3.1). Accounts are deactivated, never deleted, so security
        and order history keep their actors (F-10b).
    </p>

    <p><a href="/">Back to the home page</a></p>
</section>

@* A handful of table rules, namespaced under .admin-people. App.razor links the static wwwroot/app.css
   rather than the component-scoped bundle, so component CSS isolation is not active here; these live
   inline to avoid editing the shared stylesheet for one screen. *@
<style>
    .admin-header {
        display: flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 1rem;
        align-items: flex-start;
        justify-content: space-between;
    }

    .admin-header-actions {
        display: flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 0.5rem;
    }

    .admin-people {
        overflow-x: auto;
    }

    .admin-people table {
        width: 100%;
        border-collapse: collapse;
        font-size: 0.95rem;
    }

    .admin-people th,
    .admin-people td {
        text-align: left;
        padding: 0.6rem 0.75rem;
        border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule, #e5e5e5);
        vertical-align: top;
    }

    .admin-people thead th {
        font-weight: 600;
        color: var(--muted-foreground, #666);
        border-bottom-width: 2px;
    }

    .admin-people .person-username {
        display: block;
        font-weight: 600;
    }

    .admin-people .person-display-name {
        display: block;
        color: var(--muted-foreground, #666);
        font-size: 0.85rem;
    }

    .admin-people .muted {
        color: var(--muted-foreground, #999);
    }

    .admin-people .chip {
        display: inline-block;
        margin: 0.1rem 0.2rem 0.1rem 0;
        padding: 0.1rem 0.5rem;
        border-radius: 999px;
        font-size: 0.8rem;
        line-height: 1.5;
        background: var(--chip-background, #f0f0f0);
        color: var(--chip-foreground, #333);
        white-space: nowrap;
    }

    .admin-people .chip-role {
        text-transform: capitalize;
    }

    .admin-people .chip-ok {
        background: #e6f4ea;
        color: #1e7a37;
    }

    .admin-people .chip-warn {
        background: #fdecea;
        color: #a3261c;
    }

    .admin-people .admin-row-actions {
        white-space: nowrap;
        text-align: right;
    }

    .visually-hidden {
        position: absolute;
        width: 1px;
        height: 1px;
        margin: -1px;
        padding: 0;
        overflow: hidden;
        clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0);
        white-space: nowrap;
        border: 0;
    }
</style>

@code {
    private IReadOnlyList<PersonSummary>? _people;

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
        => _people = await People.ListPeopleAsync();

    private bool IsLockedOut(PersonSummary person)
        => person.LockoutEndAt is { } end && end > Clock.UtcNow;
}

@page "/administration/tables"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize(Policy = AuthorizationPolicies.Administration)]
@using MyRestaurant.DataAccess.Tables
@inject ITableDirectory Tables

@* The administration tables list (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §4.1, §11.4 — `area.administration`). It
   lists every table with its status and when its join secret was last rotated, links each to its
   management page, and offers table creation. Static SSR, like every administration surface, so the
   management pages it links can post full pages. Endpoint authorization enforces the policy on the
   server (anonymous → /sign-in, signed-in non-administrator → /access-denied). Not exempt from the
   obligations pipeline. Join secrets are never read or shown here (§4.1). *@

<PageTitle>Administration — tables</PageTitle>

<section class="panel">
    <div class="admin-header">
        <div>
            <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
            <h1>Tables</h1>
        </div>
        <div class="admin-header-actions">
            <a class="button-secondary" href="/administration">People</a>
            <a class="button-primary" href="/administration/tables/new">Create table</a>
        </div>
    </div>

    <p class="lede">
        Every table, oldest first. Each has a private join secret used to sign the rotating QR codes
        guests scan; the secret is never shown. Open a table to rename it, rotate its secret, or
        deactivate it.
    </p>

    @if (_tables is null)
    {
        <p class="lede">Loading…</p>
    }
    else if (_tables.Count == 0)
    {
        <p class="lede">No tables yet. Create the first one to start pairing displays and taking orders.</p>
    }
    else
    {
        <div class="admin-tables">
            <table>
                <thead>
                    <tr>
                        <th scope="col">Label</th>
                        <th scope="col">Status</th>
                        <th scope="col">Join secret rotated</th>
                        <th scope="col">Created</th>
                        <th scope="col"><span class="visually-hidden">Actions</span></th>
                    </tr>
                </thead>
                <tbody>
                    @foreach (RestaurantTableSummary table in _tables)
                    {
                        <tr>
                            <td><span class="table-label">@table.Label</span></td>
                            <td>
                                @if (table.IsActive)
                                {
                                    <span class="chip chip-ok">Active</span>
                                }
                                else
                                {
                                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Deactivated</span>
                                }
                            </td>
                            <td>
                                @if (table.JoinSecretRotatedAt is { } rotatedAt)
                                {
                                    <span>@rotatedAt.ToLocalTime().ToString("g")</span>
                                }
                                else
                                {
                                    <span class="muted">Never</span>
                                }
                            </td>
                            <td>@table.CreatedAt.ToLocalTime().ToString("g")</td>
                            <td class="admin-row-actions">
                                <a href="@($"/administration/tables/{table.TableIdentifier}")">Manage</a>
                            </td>
                        </tr>
                    }
                </tbody>
            </table>
        </div>
    }

    <p class="form-footnote">
        Deactivating a table stops its join tokens validating and its display rendering; nothing is
        deleted, so past sittings and orders keep their table. Rotating a join secret invalidates every
        outstanding QR for that table immediately.
    </p>

    <p><a href="/administration">Back to administration</a></p>
</section>

@* Table rules namespaced under .admin-tables, inline for the same reason AdministrationHome keeps
   its own: App.razor links the static stylesheet, so component CSS isolation is not active here. *@
<style>

    }

        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 0.5rem;

    .admin-tables {

@page "/administration/people/new"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize(Policy = AuthorizationPolicies.Administration)]
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Identifiers
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Security
@inject IAccountAdministration Accounts
@inject UserManager<Person> UserManager
@inject IPasswordHasher<Person> PasswordHasher
@inject IIdentifierFactory IdentifierFactory
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* Create a staff account (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.7). Administrator only (endpoint authorization,
   like every account/administration surface). Static SSR: the whole thing is a full-page form post, and
   the "temporary password" is written into the response once and never stored in the clear — the
   account is created with `must_change_password`, so the obligations pipeline forces a real password on
   the staff member's first sign-in (§3.5). Roles are granted in the same transaction that creates the
   account, each recording this administrator as grantor. Nothing is written until the form is submitted. *@

<PageTitle>Create staff account</PageTitle>

@if (_created)
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Staff account created</p>
        <h1>@_createdUsername is ready</h1>

        <p class="status-success" role="status">
            @if (_grantedRolesText is null)
            {
                <text>The account <strong>@_createdUsername</strong> was created with no role yet.</text>
            }
            else
            {
                <text>The account <strong>@_createdUsername</strong> was created with the @_grantedRolesText role.</text>
            }
        </p>

        <p class="totp-secret-label">Give this temporary password to the new staff member — it is shown only once.</p>
        <p class="totp-secret">@_temporaryPassword</p>
        <p class="lede">
            They will be asked to choose their own password the first time they sign in. If they hold the
            kitchen or administrator role, remind them to add a passkey once they are in.
        </p>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <a class="button-primary" href="@($"/administration/people/{_createdPersonIdentifier}")">Manage this account</a>
            <a class="button-secondary" href="/administration">Back to people</a>
        </div>
    </section>
}
else
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
        <h1>Create a staff account</h1>
        <p class="lede">
            Choose a username and the roles this person should hold. A temporary password is generated
            for you to hand over; they will replace it on first sign-in.
        </p>

        @if (_errorMessage is not null)
        {
            <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
        }

        <EditForm Model="Input" method="post" OnValidSubmit="CreateAsync" FormName="create-staff">
            <DataAnnotationsValidator />

            <div class="form-field">
                <label for="username">Username</label>
                <InputText id="username" @bind-Value="Input.Username" autocomplete="off"
                           autocapitalize="none" spellcheck="false" autofocus />
                <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.Username)" />
            </div>

            <div class="form-field">
                <label for="display-name">Display name <span class="optional">(optional)</span></label>
                <InputText id="display-name" @bind-Value="Input.DisplayName" autocomplete="off" />
                <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.DisplayName)" />
            </div>

            <fieldset class="form-field roles-fieldset">
                <legend>Roles</legend>
                <label class="role-option">
                    <InputCheckbox @bind-Value="Input.GrantCounter" />
                    <span><strong>Counter</strong> — close and settle sittings, adjust prices, show a table's join QR</span>
                </label>
                <label class="role-option">
                    <InputCheckbox @bind-Value="Input.GrantKitchen" />
                    <span><strong>Kitchen</strong> — fulfil lines, edit orders, activate/deactivate menu items</span>
                </label>
                <label class="role-option">
                    <InputCheckbox @bind-Value="Input.GrantAdministrator" />
                    <span><strong>Administrator</strong> — full access, including this administration area</span>
                </label>
            </fieldset>

            <div class="form-actions">
                <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Create account</button>
                <a class="button-secondary" href="/administration">Cancel</a>
            </div>
        </EditForm>

        <p class="form-footnote">
            An account with no role behaves like a guest until a role is granted. You can change roles
            later from the account's management page.
        </p>
    </section>
}

<style>
    .roles-fieldset {
        border: 1px solid var(--rule, #e5e5e5);
        border-radius: 0.5rem;
        padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
    }

    .roles-fieldset legend {
        font-weight: 600;
        padding: 0 0.35rem;
    }

    .role-option {
        display: flex;
        gap: 0.6rem;
        align-items: flex-start;
        margin: 0.4rem 0;
        font-weight: 400;
    }

    .role-option input {
        margin-top: 0.2rem;
    }
</style>

@code {
    private bool _created;
    private string? _createdUsername;
    private Guid _createdPersonIdentifier;
    private string? _temporaryPassword;
    private string? _grantedRolesText;
    private string? _errorMessage;

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm(FormName = "create-staff")]
    private CreateStaffInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    protected override void OnInitialized()
        // BL0008: a form POST binds Input itself and may assign null, so the default lives here.
        => Input ??= new();

    private async Task CreateAsync()
    {
        Person? actingAdministrator = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (actingAdministrator is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        string username = Input.Username.Trim();
        string? displayName = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Input.DisplayName) ? null : Input.DisplayName.Trim();

        // Best-effort duplicate check; the person UNIQUE constraint inside the transaction is authoritative.
        if (await UserManager.FindByNameAsync(username) is not null)
        {
            _errorMessage = "That username is already taken. Please choose another.";
            return;
        }

        List<string> roles = [];
        if (Input.GrantAdministrator)
        {
            roles.Add(RestaurantRoles.Administrator);
        }

        if (Input.GrantCounter)
        {
            roles.Add(RestaurantRoles.Counter);
        }

        if (Input.GrantKitchen)
        {
            roles.Add(RestaurantRoles.Kitchen);
        }

        Guid personIdentifier = IdentifierFactory.Create();

        // A generated temporary password, hashed immediately so the plaintext is only ever held for the
        // one response that shows it. 12 random bytes render to a 16-character URL-safe string — well
        // over the 12-character policy floor (§3.2), which the forced change re-checks anyway.
        string temporaryPassword = SecretGenerator.GenerateBase64UrlSecret(12);
        string passwordHash = PasswordHasher.HashPassword(
            new Person { PersonIdentifier = personIdentifier, Username = username },
            temporaryPassword);

        CreateStaffStatus status = await Accounts.CreateStaffAsync(
            new NewStaffAccount(personIdentifier, username, displayName, passwordHash, roles),
            actingAdministrator.PersonIdentifier,
            HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        if (status == CreateStaffStatus.UsernameTaken)
        {
            _errorMessage = "That username was taken while you were filling this in. Please choose another.";
            return;
        }

        _createdUsername = username;
        _createdPersonIdentifier = personIdentifier;
        _temporaryPassword = temporaryPassword;
        _grantedRolesText = FormatRoles(roles);
        _created = true;
    }

    private static string? FormatRoles(IReadOnlyList<string> roles) => roles.Count switch
    {
        0 => null,
        1 => roles[0],
        _ => $"{string.Join(", ", roles.Take(roles.Count - 1))} and {roles[^1]}",
    };

    private sealed class CreateStaffInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Choose a username.")]
        [StringLength(64, MinimumLength = 3, ErrorMessage = "The username must be 3–64 characters.")]
        public string Username { get; set; } = string.Empty;

        [StringLength(120, ErrorMessage = "That display name is too long.")]
        public string? DisplayName { get; set; }

        public bool GrantAdministrator { get; set; }

        public bool GrantCounter { get; set; }

        public bool GrantKitchen { get; set; }
    }
}

@page "/administration/tables/new"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize(Policy = AuthorizationPolicies.Administration)]
@using MyRestaurant.DataAccess.Tables
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Identifiers
@inject ITableAdministration Tables
@inject IIdentifierFactory IdentifierFactory
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* Create a table (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §4.1). Administrator only (endpoint authorization, like
   every administration surface). Static SSR: a full-page form post. The table is created with a
   freshly generated 32-byte join secret, held entirely server-side and never shown (§4.1). Nothing is
   written until the form is submitted; the identifier is minted here so the success screen can link
   straight to the new table's management page. *@

<PageTitle>Create table</PageTitle>

@if (_created)
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Table created</p>
        <h1>@_createdLabel is ready</h1>

        <p class="status-success" role="status">
            The table <strong>@_createdLabel</strong> was created with a private join secret. Pair a
            display to it, or show its rotating QR from the counter, to let guests join.
        </p>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <a class="button-primary" href="@($"/administration/tables/{_createdTableIdentifier}")">Manage this table</a>
            <a class="button-secondary" href="/administration/tables">Back to tables</a>
        </div>
    </section>
}
else
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
        <h1>Create a table</h1>
        <p class="lede">
            Give the table a short, unique label — this is what staff see on rosters and the kitchen
            queue. A private join secret is generated for it automatically.
        </p>

        @if (_errorMessage is not null)
        {
            <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
        }

        <EditForm Model="Input" method="post" OnValidSubmit="CreateAsync" FormName="create-table">
            <DataAnnotationsValidator />

            <div class="form-field">
                <label for="label">Label</label>
                <InputText id="label" @bind-Value="Input.Label" autocomplete="off" autofocus />
                <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.Label)" />
            </div>

            <div class="form-actions">
                <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Create table</button>
                <a class="button-secondary" href="/administration/tables">Cancel</a>
            </div>
        </EditForm>

        <p class="form-footnote">
            Labels must be unique. You can rename a table later, or deactivate it, from its management
            page.
        </p>
    </section>
}

@code {
    private bool _created;
    private string? _createdLabel;
    private Guid _createdTableIdentifier;
    private string? _errorMessage;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm(FormName = "create-table")]
    private CreateTableInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    protected override void OnInitialized()
        // BL0008: a form POST binds Input itself and may assign null, so the default lives here.
        => Input ??= new();

    private async Task CreateAsync()
    {
        string label = Input.Label.Trim();
        Guid tableIdentifier = IdentifierFactory.Create();

        CreateTableOutcome outcome = await Tables.CreateTableAsync(
            tableIdentifier, label, HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        switch (outcome)
        {
            case CreateTableOutcome.Created:
                _createdLabel = label;
                _createdTableIdentifier = tableIdentifier;
                _created = true;
                break;
            case CreateTableOutcome.LabelTaken:
                _errorMessage = "That label is already used by another table. Please choose a different one.";
                break;
        }
    }

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    private sealed class CreateTableInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Choose a label for the table.")]
        [StringLength(64, MinimumLength = 1, ErrorMessage = "The label must be 1–64 characters.")]
        public string Label { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}

@page "/administration/people/{PersonId:guid}"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize(Policy = AuthorizationPolicies.Administration)]
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Security
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Time
@inject IAccountAdministration Accounts
@inject IPersonDirectory People
@inject UserManager<Person> UserManager
@inject IPasswordHasher<Person> PasswordHasher
@inject IClock Clock
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* Manage one account (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.7). Administrator only (endpoint authorization).
   Static SSR: grant/revoke, reset, and deactivate/reactivate are full-page form posts, and the reset's
   temporary password is written into the response once and never stored in the clear. Each action runs
   through the transactional IAccountAdministration, which rotates the subject's security stamp so the
   change bites their live sessions within the revalidation window (§3.1). Successful changes redirect
   back here (post/redirect/get) carrying a one-word outcome so a refresh does not re-post; refusals
   (last administrator) are shown in place. *@

<PageTitle>@(_person is null ? "Manage account" : $"Manage {_person.Username}")</PageTitle>

@if (_person is null)
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
        <h1>Account not found</h1>
        <p class="lede">No account exists with that identifier. It may have never existed, or the link is stale.</p>
        <p><a href="/administration">Back to people</a></p>
    </section>
}
else if (_resetResult is not null)
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Credentials reset</p>
        <h1>@_person.Username can sign in again</h1>

        <p class="status-success" role="status">
            @if (_resetResult.ClearedAuthenticator)
            {
                <text>The password was reset and the authenticator was cleared. They must set a new password and enrol a new authenticator on their next sign-in.</text>
            }
            else
            {
                <text>The password was reset. They must set a new password on their next sign-in.</text>
            }
        </p>

        <p class="totp-secret-label">Give this temporary password to @_person.Username — it is shown only once.</p>
        <p class="totp-secret">@_temporaryPassword</p>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <a class="button-primary" href="@ManagePath">Back to this account</a>
            <a class="button-secondary" href="/administration">All people</a>
        </div>
    </section>
}
else
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
        <h1>@_person.Username</h1>
        @if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_person.DisplayName))
        {
            <p class="lede">@_person.DisplayName</p>
        }
        @if (_isSelf)
        {
            <p class="form-footnote">This is your own account.</p>
        }

        @if (_flashMessage is not null)
        {
            <p class="status-success" role="status">@_flashMessage</p>
        }
        @if (_errorMessage is not null)
        {
            <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
        }

        <div class="manage-facts">
            <div>
                <span class="manage-label">Status</span>
                @if (!_person.IsActive)
                {
                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Deactivated</span>
                }
                else if (IsLockedOut(_person))
                {
                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Locked until @(_person.LockoutEndAt!.Value.ToLocalTime().ToString("g"))</span>
                }
                else
                {
                    <span class="chip chip-ok">Active</span>
                }
                @if (_person.MustChangePassword)
                {
                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Must change password</span>
                }
                @if (_person.MustEnrollTotp)
                {
                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Must set up authenticator</span>
                }
            </div>
            <div>
                <span class="manage-label">Roles</span>
                @if (_person.Roles.Count == 0)
                {
                    <span class="muted">None (guest)</span>
                }
                else
                {
                    @foreach (string role in _person.Roles)
                    {
                        <span class="chip chip-role">@role</span>
                    }
                }
            </div>
            <div>
                <span class="manage-label">Credentials</span>
                @if (_person.HasPassword)
                {
                    <span class="chip">Password</span>
                }
                @if (_person.HasAuthenticator)
                {
                    <span class="chip">Authenticator</span>
                }
                @if (!_person.HasPassword && !_person.HasAuthenticator)
                {
                    <span class="muted">None</span>
                }
            </div>
        </div>

        <hr class="manage-rule" />

        <h2 class="manage-heading">Roles</h2>
        @if (_grantableRoles.Count > 0)
        {
            <EditForm Model="GrantInput" method="post" OnValidSubmit="GrantRoleAsync" FormName="manage-grant-role" class="manage-inline-form">
                <AntiforgeryToken />
                <label class="visually-hidden" for="grant-role">Role to grant</label>
                <InputSelect id="grant-role" @bind-Value="GrantInput.RoleName">
                    <option value="">Choose a role…</option>
                    @foreach (string role in _grantableRoles)
                    {
                        <option value="@role">@Capitalize(role)</option>
                    }
                </InputSelect>
                <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Grant role</button>
            </EditForm>
        }
        else
        {
            <p class="form-footnote">This account already holds every role.</p>
        }

        @if (_person.Roles.Count > 0)
        {
            <EditForm Model="RevokeInput" method="post" OnValidSubmit="RevokeRoleAsync" FormName="manage-revoke-role" class="manage-inline-form">
                <AntiforgeryToken />
                <label class="visually-hidden" for="revoke-role">Role to revoke</label>
                <InputSelect id="revoke-role" @bind-Value="RevokeInput.RoleName">
                    <option value="">Choose a role…</option>
                    @foreach (string role in _person.Roles)
                    {
                        <option value="@role">@Capitalize(role)</option>
                    }
                </InputSelect>
                <button type="submit" class="link-button danger">Revoke role</button>
            </EditForm>
        }

        <hr class="manage-rule" />

        <h2 class="manage-heading">Credentials</h2>
        <p class="lede">
            Reset generates a temporary password (shown once) and forces a password change on next
            sign-in. If an authenticator is enrolled it is cleared too, forcing re-enrolment.
        </p>
        <form method="post" @formname="manage-reset-credentials" @onsubmit="ResetCredentialsAsync">
            <AntiforgeryToken />
            <div class="form-actions">
                <button type="submit" class="button-secondary">Reset credentials</button>
            </div>
        </form>

        <hr class="manage-rule" />

        <h2 class="manage-heading">Account</h2>
        @if (_person.IsActive)
        {
            <p class="lede">
                Deactivating blocks sign-in and ends the account's live sessions. Nothing is deleted, so
                security and order history keep their actor (F-10b); you can reactivate at any time.
            </p>
            <form method="post" @formname="manage-set-active" @onsubmit="ToggleActivationAsync">
                <AntiforgeryToken />
                <div class="form-actions">
                    <button type="submit" class="link-button danger">Deactivate account</button>
                </div>
            </form>
        }
        else
        {
            <p class="lede">This account is deactivated and cannot sign in. Reactivate it to restore access.</p>
            <form method="post" @formname="manage-set-active" @onsubmit="ToggleActivationAsync">
                <AntiforgeryToken />
                <div class="form-actions">
                    <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Reactivate account</button>
                </div>
            </form>
        }

        <p class="manage-back"><a href="/administration">Back to people</a></p>
    </section>
}

<style>
    .manage-facts {
        display: grid;
        gap: 0.75rem;
        margin: 1rem 0;

@code {
    private static readonly string[] AllStaffRoles =
        [RestaurantRoles.Administrator, RestaurantRoles.Counter, RestaurantRoles.Kitchen];

    private PersonSummary? _person;
    private IReadOnlyList<string> _grantableRoles = [];
    private bool _isSelf;
    private string? _flashMessage;
    private string? _errorMessage;

    private CredentialResetResult? _resetResult;
    private string? _temporaryPassword;

    [Parameter]
    public Guid PersonId { get; set; }

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm(FormName = "manage-grant-role")]
    private RoleSelectionModel GrantInput { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm(FormName = "manage-revoke-role")]
    private RoleSelectionModel RevokeInput { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? Done { get; set; }

    private string ManagePath => $"/administration/people/{PersonId}";

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // BL0008: form POSTs bind these themselves and may assign null, so defaults live here.
        GrantInput ??= new();
        RevokeInput ??= new();

        _person = await People.GetPersonAsync(PersonId, HttpContext.RequestAborted);
        if (_person is null)
        {
            return;
        }

        _grantableRoles = AllStaffRoles.Where(role => !_person.Roles.Contains(role)).ToArray();

        Person? actingAdministrator = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        _isSelf = actingAdministrator is not null && actingAdministrator.PersonIdentifier == _person.PersonIdentifier;

        // Render the flash for a completed action after the post/redirect/get, on GET only.
        if (HttpContext.Request.Method == HttpMethods.Get)
        {
            _flashMessage = FlashFor(Done);
        }
    }

    private async Task GrantRoleAsync()
    {
        if (_person is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration");
            return;
        }

        Person? admin = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (admin is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(GrantInput.RoleName))
        {
            _errorMessage = "Choose a role to grant.";
            return;
        }

        RoleGrantOutcome outcome = await Accounts.GrantRoleAsync(
            _person.PersonIdentifier, GrantInput.RoleName, admin.PersonIdentifier, HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        switch (outcome)
        {
            case RoleGrantOutcome.Granted:
                RedirectWithOutcome("role-granted");
                break;
            case RoleGrantOutcome.AlreadyHeld:
                RedirectWithOutcome("role-already-held");
                break;
            case RoleGrantOutcome.PersonNotFound:
                Navigation.NavigateTo(ManagePath);
                break;
        }
    }

    private async Task RevokeRoleAsync()
    {
        if (_person is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration");
            return;
        }

        Person? admin = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (admin is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(RevokeInput.RoleName))
        {
            _errorMessage = "Choose a role to revoke.";
            return;
        }

        RoleRevokeOutcome outcome = await Accounts.RevokeRoleAsync(
            _person.PersonIdentifier, RevokeInput.RoleName, admin.PersonIdentifier, HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        switch (outcome)
        {
            case RoleRevokeOutcome.Revoked:
                RedirectWithOutcome("role-revoked");
                break;
            case RoleRevokeOutcome.NotHeld:
                RedirectWithOutcome("role-not-held");
                break;
            case RoleRevokeOutcome.WouldRemoveLastAdministrator:
                _errorMessage = "This is the only administrator, so the administrator role cannot be removed. "
                    + "Grant administrator to someone else first.";
                break;
            case RoleRevokeOutcome.PersonNotFound:
                Navigation.NavigateTo(ManagePath);
                break;
        }
    }

    private async Task ResetCredentialsAsync()
    {
        if (_person is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration");
            return;
        }

        Person? admin = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (admin is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        // Generate + hash the temporary password here so the plaintext is only ever held for the one
        // response that shows it (§3.2). 12 random bytes → a 16-character URL-safe string.
        string temporaryPassword = SecretGenerator.GenerateBase64UrlSecret(12);
        string passwordHash = PasswordHasher.HashPassword(
            new Person { PersonIdentifier = _person.PersonIdentifier, Username = _person.Username },
            temporaryPassword);

        CredentialResetResult result = await Accounts.ResetCredentialsAsync(
            _person.PersonIdentifier, passwordHash, admin.PersonIdentifier, HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        if (result.Outcome == CredentialResetOutcome.PersonNotFound)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(ManagePath);
            return;
        }

        // Show the temporary password once, in this response (no redirect).
        _temporaryPassword = temporaryPassword;
        _resetResult = result;
    }

    private async Task ToggleActivationAsync()
    {
        if (_person is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration");
            return;
        }

        Person? admin = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (admin is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        bool target = !_person.IsActive;
        AccountActivationOutcome outcome = await Accounts.SetAccountActiveAsync(
            _person.PersonIdentifier, target, admin.PersonIdentifier, HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        switch (outcome)
        {
            case AccountActivationOutcome.Changed:
                RedirectWithOutcome(target ? "account-reactivated" : "account-deactivated");
                break;
            case AccountActivationOutcome.NoChange:
                Navigation.NavigateTo(ManagePath);
                break;
            case AccountActivationOutcome.WouldDeactivateLastAdministrator:
                _errorMessage = "This is the only administrator that can sign in, so it cannot be deactivated. "
                    + "Make sure another administrator is active first.";
                break;
            case AccountActivationOutcome.PersonNotFound:
                Navigation.NavigateTo(ManagePath);
                break;
        }
    }

    private void RedirectWithOutcome(string outcome)
        => Navigation.NavigateTo($"{ManagePath}?done={outcome}");

    private static string? FlashFor(string? outcome) => outcome switch
    {
        "role-granted" => "Role granted. It takes effect on their sessions within a few minutes.",
        "role-already-held" => "That role was already held — nothing changed.",
        "role-revoked" => "Role revoked. It takes effect on their sessions within a few minutes.",
        "role-not-held" => "That role was not held — nothing changed.",
        "account-deactivated" => "Account deactivated. Its sessions end within a few minutes.",
        "account-reactivated" => "Account reactivated.",
        _ => null,
    };

    private bool IsLockedOut(PersonSummary person)
        => person.LockoutEndAt is { } end && end > Clock.UtcNow;

    private static string Capitalize(string role)
        => string.IsNullOrEmpty(role) ? role : char.ToUpperInvariant(role[0]) + role[1..];

    private sealed class RoleSelectionModel
    {
        public string RoleName { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}
