@page "/administration/tables/{TableId:guid}"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize(Policy = AuthorizationPolicies.Administration)]
@using MyRestaurant.DataAccess.Tables
@inject ITableDirectory TableDirectory
@inject ITableAdministration Tables
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* Manage one table (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §4.1). Administrator only (endpoint authorization).
   Static SSR: rename, rotate-secret, and deactivate/reactivate are full-page form posts. Successful
   changes redirect back here (post/redirect/get) carrying a one-word outcome so a refresh does not
   re-post; a taken label is shown in place. The join secret is never read or shown — rotation replaces
   it server-side (§4.1). A link to the join-code fallback (§4.5) sits alongside, for when a table's
   display is down. *@

<PageTitle>@(_table is null ? "Manage table" : $"Manage {_table.Label}")</PageTitle>

@if (_table is null)
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
        <h1>Table not found</h1>
        <p class="lede">No table exists with that identifier. It may have never existed, or the link is stale.</p>
        <p><a href="/administration/tables">Back to tables</a></p>
    </section>
}
else
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
        <h1>@_table.Label</h1>

        @if (_flashMessage is not null)
        {
            <p class="status-success" role="status">@_flashMessage</p>
        }
        @if (_errorMessage is not null)
        {
            <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
        }

        <div class="manage-facts">
            <div>
                <span class="manage-label">Status</span>
                @if (_table.IsActive)
                {
                    <span class="chip chip-ok">Active</span>
                }
                else
                {
                    <span class="chip chip-warn">Deactivated</span>
                }
            </div>
            <div>
                <span class="manage-label">Join secret rotated</span>
                @if (_table.JoinSecretRotatedAt is { } rotatedAt)
                {
                    <span>@rotatedAt.ToLocalTime().ToString("g")</span>
                }
                else
                {
                    <span class="muted">Never</span>
                }
            </div>
            <div>
                <span class="manage-label">Created</span>
                <span>@_table.CreatedAt.ToLocalTime().ToString("g")</span>
            </div>
        </div>

        <hr class="manage-rule" />

        <h2 class="manage-heading">Label</h2>
        <EditForm Model="RenameInput" method="post" OnValidSubmit="RenameAsync" FormName="manage-rename" class="manage-inline-form">
            <DataAnnotationsValidator />
            <label class="visually-hidden" for="rename-label">New label</label>
            <InputText id="rename-label" @bind-Value="RenameInput.Label" autocomplete="off" />
            <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Rename</button>
            <ValidationMessage For="@(() => RenameInput.Label)" />
        </EditForm>

        <hr class="manage-rule" />

        <h2 class="manage-heading">Join code</h2>
        @if (_table.IsActive)
        {
            <p class="lede">
                Guests join by scanning this table's rotating QR code. Its own display shows it; if that
                display is down, show the same code here and let the guest scan your screen.
            </p>
            <div class="form-actions">
                <a class="button-secondary" href="@($"/administration/tables/{TableId}/join-code")">Show join code</a>
            </div>
        }
        else
        {
            <p class="lede">
                This table is deactivated, so it renders no join code and its tokens do not validate.
                Reactivate it below to show a code again.
            </p>
        }

        <hr class="manage-rule" />

        <h2 class="manage-heading">Join secret</h2>
        <p class="lede">
            The join secret signs the rotating QR codes guests scan. Rotating it generates a new secret
            and <strong>immediately invalidates every outstanding QR</strong> for this table; paired
            displays re-render with the new one on their own. Do this if a code may have leaked.
        </p>
        <form method="post" @formname="manage-rotate-secret" @onsubmit="RotateSecretAsync">
            <AntiforgeryToken />
            <div class="form-actions">
                <button type="submit" class="button-secondary">Rotate join secret</button>
            </div>
        </form>

        <hr class="manage-rule" />

        <h2 class="manage-heading">Table</h2>
        @if (_table.IsActive)
        {
            <p class="lede">
                Deactivating stops this table's join tokens validating and its display rendering.
                Nothing is deleted, so past sittings and orders keep their table; you can reactivate at
                any time.
            </p>
            <form method="post" @formname="manage-set-active" @onsubmit="ToggleActivationAsync">
                <AntiforgeryToken />
                <div class="form-actions">
                    <button type="submit" class="link-button danger">Deactivate table</button>
                </div>
            </form>
        }
        else
        {
            <p class="lede">This table is deactivated. Reactivate it to let its tokens validate and its display render again.</p>
            <form method="post" @formname="manage-set-active" @onsubmit="ToggleActivationAsync">
                <AntiforgeryToken />
                <div class="form-actions">
                    <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Reactivate table</button>
                </div>
            </form>
        }

        <p class="manage-back"><a href="/administration/tables">Back to tables</a></p>
    </section>
}

        white-space: nowrap;
        border: 0;
    }

@code {
    private RestaurantTableSummary? _table;
    private string? _flashMessage;
    private string? _errorMessage;

    [Parameter]
    public Guid TableId { get; set; }

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm(FormName = "manage-rename")]
    private RenameInputModel RenameInput { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? Done { get; set; }

    private string ManagePath => $"/administration/tables/{TableId}";

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // BL0008: a form POST binds RenameInput itself and may assign null, so the default lives here.
        RenameInput ??= new();

        _table = await TableDirectory.GetTableAsync(TableId, HttpContext.RequestAborted);
        if (_table is null)
        {
            return;
        }

        // On GET, pre-fill the rename box with the current label and render any post/redirect flash.
        // On POST the submitted value is already bound, so it must not be overwritten.
        if (HttpContext.Request.Method == HttpMethods.Get)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(RenameInput.Label))
            {
                RenameInput.Label = _table.Label;
            }

            _flashMessage = FlashFor(Done);
        }
    }

    private async Task RenameAsync()
    {
        if (_table is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration/tables");
            return;
        }

        RenameTableOutcome outcome = await Tables.RenameTableAsync(
            TableId, RenameInput.Label.Trim(), HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        switch (outcome)
        {
            case RenameTableOutcome.Renamed:
                RedirectWithOutcome("renamed");
                break;
            case RenameTableOutcome.NoChange:
                RedirectWithOutcome("rename-no-change");
                break;
            case RenameTableOutcome.LabelTaken:
                _errorMessage = "Another table already uses that label. Please choose a different one.";
                break;
            case RenameTableOutcome.TableNotFound:
                Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration/tables");
                break;
        }
    }

    private async Task RotateSecretAsync()
    {
        if (_table is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration/tables");
            return;
        }

        RotateJoinSecretOutcome outcome = await Tables.RotateJoinSecretAsync(TableId, HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        switch (outcome)
        {
            case RotateJoinSecretOutcome.Rotated:
                RedirectWithOutcome("secret-rotated");
                break;
            case RotateJoinSecretOutcome.TableNotFound:
                Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration/tables");
                break;
        }
    }

    private async Task ToggleActivationAsync()
    {
        if (_table is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration/tables");
            return;
        }

        bool target = !_table.IsActive;
        TableActivationOutcome outcome = await Tables.SetTableActiveAsync(TableId, target, HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        switch (outcome)
        {
            case TableActivationOutcome.Changed:
                RedirectWithOutcome(target ? "reactivated" : "deactivated");
                break;
            case TableActivationOutcome.NoChange:
                Navigation.NavigateTo(ManagePath);
                break;
            case TableActivationOutcome.TableNotFound:
                Navigation.NavigateTo("/administration/tables");
                break;
        }
    }

    private void RedirectWithOutcome(string outcome)
        => Navigation.NavigateTo($"{ManagePath}?done={outcome}");

    private static string? FlashFor(string? outcome) => outcome switch
    {
        "renamed" => "Table renamed.",
        "rename-no-change" => "That was already the label — nothing changed.",
        "secret-rotated" => "Join secret rotated. Every outstanding QR for this table has stopped working; "
            + "paired displays pick up the new one automatically.",
        "deactivated" => "Table deactivated. Its join tokens no longer validate and its display stops rendering.",
        "reactivated" => "Table reactivated.",
        _ => null,
    };

    private sealed class RenameInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter a label.")]
        [StringLength(64, MinimumLength = 1, ErrorMessage = "The label must be 1–64 characters.")]
        public string Label { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}

@page "/administration/tables/{TableId:guid}/join-code"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize(Policy = AuthorizationPolicies.Administration)]
@using MyRestaurant.DataAccess.Tables
@using MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Tables
@inject ITableDirectory TableDirectory
@inject ITableJoinTokens JoinTokens
@inject RestaurantOptions Options

@* The administrator/counter join-code fallback (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §4.5, §11.4). When a table's
   display device is dead, staff can render the *same* rotating QR here on demand; the guest scans this
   screen. The join secret never leaves the server — this page shows only the server-rendered QR and the
   scan URL it encodes (§4.1/§4.5).

   Static SSR, like every administration surface: it sets no cookie and simply reads and renders. It is
   therefore a snapshot of the current window — a scanned code keeps working for the current *and*
   previous window (worst case ~2× the rotation, §4.3), so it will not die mid-scan, and a "Show a fresh
   code" link re-renders the next window on demand. The window-aligned auto-refresh belongs to the paired
   display surface (§11.5) and lands with it; here a manual refresh is the operational fallback. A
   deactivated or unknown table renders no code (§4.1). *@

<PageTitle>@(_table is null ? "Join code" : $"Join code — {_table.Label}")</PageTitle>

@if (_table is null)
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
        <h1>Table not found</h1>
        <p class="lede">No table exists with that identifier. It may have never existed, or the link is stale.</p>
        <p><a href="/administration/tables">Back to tables</a></p>
    </section>
}
else if (!_table.IsActive)
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
        <h1>@_table.Label</h1>
        <p class="lede">
            This table is deactivated, so its join code does not render and its tokens do not validate
            (§4.1). Reactivate the table to show a code again.
        </p>
        <p class="join-code-back"><a href="@ManagePath">Back to @_table.Label</a></p>
    </section>
}
else if (_qr is null)
{
    @* Active table but no code came back — treat as a transient condition rather than dereference null. *@
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration</p>
        <h1>@_table.Label</h1>
        <p class="status-error" role="alert">The join code could not be generated just now. Try again.</p>
        <p class="join-code-back"><a href="@ManagePath">Back to @_table.Label</a></p>
    </section>
}
else
{
    <section class="panel account-panel join-code-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Administration — join code</p>
        <h1>@_table.Label</h1>
        <p class="lede">
            A guest scans this code to join @_table.Label. Use this when the table's own display is down —
            it is the same rotating code the display shows (§4.5). The private join secret never leaves the
            server.
        </p>

        <div class="join-qr">
            @((MarkupString)_qr.QrCodeSvg)
        </div>

        <p class="join-code-url-label">The code links to:</p>
        <p class="join-code-url">@_qr.JoinUrl</p>

        <p class="join-code-rotation">
            Rotates every @Options.TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds seconds — next at
            <strong>@_qr.NextRotationAt.ToLocalTime().ToString("T")</strong>
            (about @SecondsUntilRotation second@(SecondsUntilRotation == 1 ? "" : "s") from now). A code
            that has just been scanned keeps working for up to about @(Options.TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds * 2)
            seconds, so it will not stop working in a guest's hand.
        </p>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <a class="button-primary" href="@JoinCodePath">Show a fresh code</a>
            <a class="button-secondary" href="@ManagePath">Back to @_table.Label</a>
        </div>
    </section>
}

@* Namespaced under .join-code-* and inline for the same reason the other administration pages keep
   their CSS inline: App.razor links the static stylesheet, so component CSS isolation is not active. *@
<style>
    .join-code-panel .join-qr {
        display: flex;
        justify-content: center;
        margin: 1.25rem 0;
    }

    .join-code-panel .join-qr-svg {
        width: 100%;
        max-width: 20rem;
        height: auto;
        border-radius: 0.5rem;
        border: 1px solid var(--rule, #e5e5e5);
    }

    .join-code-url-label {
        margin: 0.75rem 0 0.25rem;
        font-size: 0.85rem;
        color: var(--muted-foreground, #666);
    }

    .join-code-url {
        margin: 0 0 1rem;
        padding: 0.5rem 0.65rem;
        border-radius: 0.4rem;
        background: var(--surface-sunken, #f4f4f5);
        font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
        font-size: 0.85rem;
        word-break: break-all;
    }

    .join-code-rotation {
        color: var(--muted-foreground, #555);
        font-size: 0.9rem;
        margin: 0 0 1.25rem;
    }

    .join-code-back {
        margin-top: 1.5rem;
    }
</style>

@code {
    private RestaurantTableSummary? _table;
    private TableJoinQrCode? _qr;

    [Parameter]
    public Guid TableId { get; set; }

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    private string ManagePath => $"/administration/tables/{TableId}";

    private string JoinCodePath => $"/administration/tables/{TableId}/join-code";

    private int SecondsUntilRotation => _qr is null
        ? 0
        : Math.Max(0, (int)Math.Ceiling((_qr.NextRotationAt - _qr.GeneratedAt).TotalSeconds));

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        _table = await TableDirectory.GetTableAsync(TableId, HttpContext.RequestAborted);
        if (_table is null || !_table.IsActive)
        {
            return;
        }

        _qr = await JoinTokens.DescribeCurrentAsync(TableId, HttpContext.RequestAborted);
    }
}

@code {
    private bool _needsSetup;

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // Point a fresh deployment at /setup while no administrator exists (§3.6). Best-effort: the
        // landing page must still render if the database is briefly unreachable (that is what
        // /healthz/ready is for), so a failure here simply hides the pointer.
        try
        {
            _needsSetup = !await Bootstrap.AdministratorExistsAsync();
        }
        catch (Exception exception) when (exception is not OperationCanceledException)
        {
            _needsSetup = false;
        }
    }
}

@page "/setup"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@using System.Text
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Identifiers
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Security
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Time
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection
@inject IFirstAdministratorBootstrap Bootstrap
@inject IPasswordHasher<Person> PasswordHasher
@inject SignInManager<Person> SignInManager
@inject UserManager<Person> UserManager
@inject IIdentifierFactory IdentifierFactory
@inject IClock Clock
@inject IDataProtectionProvider DataProtection
@inject RestaurantOptions Options
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* The first-administrator bootstrap wizard (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.6). Reachable only while ZERO
   administrators exist; the moment one does, this page returns 404. Anonymous by design — there is no
   one to sign in as yet (the chicken-and-egg the whole feature resolves). Static SSR, like every
   account surface, because it writes cookies on the response (the accumulating setup ticket, and the
   authentication cookie once the administrator is created).

   The wizard is a single page driven by a Data-Protection-protected cookie (SetupTicket): collect
   account details → register a passkey → enroll TOTP → review and commit. The passkey and TOTP steps
   run their real ceremonies and cannot be skipped; nothing touches the database until the final step
   writes the whole account — person, passkey, TOTP secret, ten recovery codes, and the self-granted
   administrator role — in one advisory-locked transaction (FirstAdministratorBootstrap). *@

<PageTitle>Set up @Options.RestaurantName</PageTitle>

@if (_adminExists)
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Setup</p>
        <h1>Setup is already complete</h1>
        <p class="lede">
            An administrator already exists for @Options.RestaurantName, so first-run setup is closed.
            If you are staff, sign in as usual.
        </p>
        <p><a href="@AccountRoutes.SignIn">Go to sign in</a></p>
    </section>
}
else if (_completed && _recoveryCodes is not null)
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">Setup complete</p>
        <h1>You are the administrator</h1>
        <p class="status-success" role="status">
            The administrator account <strong>@_completedUsername</strong> was created and you are now
            signed in.
        </p>

        <p class="totp-secret-label">Save these recovery codes now — this is the only time they are shown.</p>
        <p class="lede">
            Each works once, in place of your authenticator code, on the password sign-in. Keep them
            somewhere safe and offline.
        </p>
        <ul class="recovery-codes">
            @foreach (string recoveryCode in _recoveryCodes)
            {
                <li>@recoveryCode</li>
            }
        </ul>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <a class="button-primary" href="/administration">Go to administration</a>
            <a class="button-secondary" href="/">Home</a>
        </div>
    </section>
}
else
{
    <section class="panel account-panel">
        <p class="eyebrow">First-run setup</p>

        <ol class="setup-steps" aria-label="Setup progress">
            <li class="@StepClass(0)">Account</li>
            <li class="@StepClass(1)">Passkey</li>
            <li class="@StepClass(2)">Authenticator</li>
            <li class="@StepClass(3)">Finish</li>
        </ol>

        @if (_errorMessage is not null)
        {
            <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
        }

        @if (_ticket is null)
        {
            @* Step 1 — account details. *@
            <h1>Create the first administrator</h1>
            <p class="lede">
                No administrator exists yet. Set one up now: choose a username and password, then add a
                passkey and an authenticator. All three are required — the passkey and the authenticator
                cannot be skipped.
            </p>

            <EditForm Model="AccountInput" method="post" OnValidSubmit="StartAsync" FormName="setup-account">
                <DataAnnotationsValidator />

                <div class="form-field">
                    <label for="username">Username</label>
                    <InputText id="username" @bind-Value="AccountInput.Username" autocomplete="username"
                               autocapitalize="none" spellcheck="false" autofocus />
                    <ValidationMessage For="@(() => AccountInput.Username)" />
                </div>

                <div class="form-field">
                    <label for="display-name">Display name <span class="optional">(optional)</span></label>
                    <InputText id="display-name" @bind-Value="AccountInput.DisplayName" autocomplete="name" />
                    <ValidationMessage For="@(() => AccountInput.DisplayName)" />
                </div>

                <div class="form-field">
                    <label for="password">Password</label>
                    <InputText id="password" type="password" @bind-Value="AccountInput.Password"
                               autocomplete="new-password" />
                    <ValidationMessage For="@(() => AccountInput.Password)" />
                </div>

                <div class="form-field">
                    <label for="confirm-password">Confirm password</label>
                    <InputText id="confirm-password" type="password" @bind-Value="AccountInput.ConfirmPassword"
                               autocomplete="new-password" />
                    <ValidationMessage For="@(() => AccountInput.ConfirmPassword)" />
                </div>

                <div class="form-actions">
                    <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Continue to passkey</button>
                </div>
            </EditForm>

            <p class="form-footnote">
                Use at least 12 characters. Your operating system's password manager can generate and
                store a strong one for you.
            </p>
        }
        else if (_ticket.Step == SetupStep.Passkey)
        {
            @* Step 2 — passkey registration (WebAuthn attestation). *@
            <h1>Add a passkey</h1>
            <p class="lede">
                A passkey lets you sign in with your fingerprint, face, or device PIN, and it cannot be
                phished. Register one now for <strong>@_ticket.Username</strong>. You can add more, and
                rename this one, later.
            </p>

            <form class="passkey-add" @formname="setup-passkey" method="post" @onsubmit="RegisterPasskeyAsync">
                <AntiforgeryToken />
                <PasskeySubmit Operation="PasskeyOperation.Create" Name="PasskeyInput"
                               CreationOptionsUrl="@AccountRoutes.SetupPasskeyCreationOptions" class="button-primary">
                    Register a passkey
                </PasskeySubmit>
            </form>
        }
        else if (_ticket.Step == SetupStep.Totp)
        {
            @* Step 3 — authenticator (TOTP) enrollment. *@
            <h1>Set up your authenticator</h1>
            <p class="lede">
                Scan this QR code with an authenticator app (or type the key), then enter the six-digit
                code it shows. This is your second factor when you sign in with a password.
            </p>

            <div class="totp-qr">
                @((MarkupString)_qrCodeSvg)
            </div>

            <p class="totp-secret-label">Can't scan? Enter this key manually:</p>
            <p class="totp-secret">@_manualEntrySecret</p>

            <EditForm Model="TotpInput" method="post" OnValidSubmit="ConfirmTotpAsync" FormName="setup-totp">
                <DataAnnotationsValidator />

                <div class="form-field">
                    <label for="code">Six-digit code</label>
                    <InputText id="code" @bind-Value="TotpInput.Code" autocomplete="one-time-code"
                               inputmode="numeric" autocapitalize="none" spellcheck="false" autofocus />
                    <ValidationMessage For="@(() => TotpInput.Code)" />
                </div>

                <div class="form-actions">
                    <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Confirm and review</button>
                </div>
            </EditForm>
        }
        else
        {
            @* Step 4 — review and commit. *@
            <h1>Review and finish</h1>
            <p class="lede">
                Everything is ready. Creating the administrator writes the account, your passkey, your
                authenticator, and your recovery codes together, and grants the administrator role.
            </p>

            <dl class="setup-summary">
                <div>
                    <dt>Username</dt>
                    <dd>@_ticket.Username</dd>
                </div>
                <div>
                    <dt>Display name</dt>
                    <dd>@(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_ticket.DisplayName) ? "—" : _ticket.DisplayName)</dd>
                </div>
                <div>
                    <dt>Passkey</dt>
                    <dd>Registered</dd>
                </div>
                <div>
                    <dt>Authenticator</dt>
                    <dd>Confirmed</dd>
                </div>
            </dl>

            <form @formname="setup-finish" method="post" @onsubmit="FinishAsync">
                <AntiforgeryToken />
                <div class="form-actions">
                    <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Create administrator</button>
                </div>
            </form>
        }

        @if (_ticket is not null)
        {
            <form @formname="setup-restart" method="post" @onsubmit="RestartAsync" class="setup-restart">
                <AntiforgeryToken />
                <button type="submit" class="link-button">Start over</button>
            </form>
        }
    </section>
}

@code {
    private SetupTicketProtector _protector = default!;
    private SetupTicket? _ticket;
    private bool _adminExists;
    private bool _completed;
    private string? _completedUsername;
    private IReadOnlyList<string>? _recoveryCodes;
    private string? _errorMessage;

    // Prepared once for the TOTP step so the same QR survives a mistyped-code re-render.
    private string _qrCodeSvg = string.Empty;
    private string _manualEntrySecret = string.Empty;

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm(FormName = "setup-account")]
    private AccountInputModel AccountInput { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm(FormName = "setup-passkey")]
    private PasskeyInputModel PasskeyInput { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm(FormName = "setup-totp")]
    private TotpInputModel TotpInput { get; set; } = default!;

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // BL0008: a form POST binds these itself and may assign null, so defaults live here.
        AccountInput ??= new();
        PasskeyInput ??= new();
        TotpInput ??= new();
        _protector = new SetupTicketProtector(DataProtection);

        // The unlocked gate: if any administrator exists, /setup is gone (§3.6). The authoritative
        // re-check happens under the advisory lock at commit, so a race here is harmless.
        _adminExists = await Bootstrap.AdministratorExistsAsync(HttpContext.RequestAborted);
        if (_adminExists)
        {
            HttpContext.Response.StatusCode = StatusCodes.Status404NotFound;
            return;
        }

        if (HttpContext.Request.Cookies.TryGetValue(SetupCookie.Name, out string? cookie)
            && _protector.TryUnprotect(cookie, out SetupTicket? ticket)
            && ticket is not null
            && !ticket.HasExpired(Clock.UtcNow, SetupCookie.Lifetime))
        {
            _ticket = ticket;
            if (_ticket.Step == SetupStep.Totp && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(_ticket.TotpSecretBase32))
            {
                PrepareTotpDisplay(_ticket);
            }
        }
        else if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(cookie))
        {
            // Stale, tampered, or from another key: drop it and start clean.
            SetupCookie.Delete(HttpContext.Response);
            _errorMessage = "Your setup session expired. Please start again.";
        }
    }

    private async Task StartAsync()
    {
        if (_adminExists)
        {
            return;
        }

        string username = AccountInput.Username.Trim();
        string? displayName = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(AccountInput.DisplayName)
            ? null
            : AccountInput.DisplayName.Trim();

        // Best-effort duplicate check; the person UNIQUE constraint and the commit are authoritative.
        if (await UserManager.FindByNameAsync(username) is not null)
        {
            _errorMessage = "That username is already taken. Please choose another.";
            return;
        }

        Guid personIdentifier = IdentifierFactory.Create();

        // Hash immediately so the plaintext is never carried in the ticket. The hasher ignores the
        // user argument (no per-user pepper, §3.2); a throwaway carrier is fine.
        string passwordHash = PasswordHasher.HashPassword(
            new Person { PersonIdentifier = personIdentifier, Username = username },
            AccountInput.Password);

        WriteTicketAndReload(new SetupTicket(
            personIdentifier,
            Clock.UtcNow,
            SetupStep.Passkey,
            username,
            displayName,
            passwordHash,
            Passkey: null,
            TotpSecretBase32: null));
    }

    private async Task RegisterPasskeyAsync()
    {
        if (_adminExists)
        {
            return;
        }

        if (_ticket is null || _ticket.Step != SetupStep.Passkey)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.Setup);
            return;
        }

        if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(PasskeyInput.Error))
        {
            _errorMessage = "That passkey could not be added. Please try again.";
            return;
        }

        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(PasskeyInput.CredentialJson))
        {
            _errorMessage = "Your browser did not provide a passkey.";
            return;
        }

        PasskeyAttestationResult attestation =
            await SignInManager.PerformPasskeyAttestationAsync(PasskeyInput.CredentialJson);
        if (!attestation.Succeeded || attestation.Passkey is null)
        {
            _errorMessage = "That passkey could not be registered. Please try again.";
            return;
        }

        UserPasskeyInfo info = attestation.Passkey;
        SetupPasskey passkey = new(
            info.CredentialId,
            info.PublicKey,
            (long)info.SignCount,
            info.Transports,
            Name: $"Passkey added {Clock.UtcNow.ToLocalTime():d MMM yyyy}",
            info.IsUserVerified,
            info.IsBackupEligible,
            info.IsBackedUp);

        // Mint the authenticator secret now so the TOTP step's QR is stable across re-renders.
        string totpSecretBase32 = Base32Text.Encode(Rfc6238Totp.GenerateSecret());

        WriteTicketAndReload(_ticket with
        {
            Step = SetupStep.Totp,
            Passkey = passkey,
            TotpSecretBase32 = totpSecretBase32,
        });
    }

    private Task ConfirmTotpAsync()
    {
        if (_adminExists)
        {
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        if (_ticket is null || _ticket.Step != SetupStep.Totp || string.IsNullOrEmpty(_ticket.TotpSecretBase32))
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.Setup);
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        if (!Base32Text.TryDecode(_ticket.TotpSecretBase32, out byte[] secret))
        {
            SetupCookie.Delete(HttpContext.Response);
            _ticket = null;
            _errorMessage = "Something went wrong with the setup code. Please start again.";
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        string normalized = (TotpInput.Code ?? string.Empty).Replace(" ", string.Empty).Replace("-", string.Empty);
        if (!Rfc6238Totp.ValidateCode(secret, normalized, Clock.UtcNow))
        {
            // The QR was already prepared in OnInitializedAsync (step is still Totp); it re-renders.
            _errorMessage = "That code did not match. Check your authenticator and try again.";
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        WriteTicketAndReload(_ticket with { Step = SetupStep.Review });
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }

    private async Task FinishAsync()
    {
        if (_adminExists)
        {
            return;
        }

        if (_ticket is null
            || _ticket.Step != SetupStep.Review
            || _ticket.Passkey is null
            || string.IsNullOrEmpty(_ticket.TotpSecretBase32))
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.Setup);
            return;
        }

        SetupPasskey stored = _ticket.Passkey;
        UserPasskeyInfo passkey = new(
            stored.CredentialId,
            stored.PublicKey,
            Clock.UtcNow, // placeholder; the bootstrap stamps created_at itself
            (uint)stored.SignatureCounter,
            stored.Transports,
            stored.IsUserVerified,
            stored.IsBackupEligible,
            stored.IsBackedUp,
            attestationObject: [],
            clientDataJson: [])
        {
            Name = stored.Name,
        };

        FirstAdministratorBootstrapResult result = await Bootstrap.CreateFirstAdministratorAsync(
            new NewAdministrator(
                _ticket.PersonIdentifier,
                _ticket.Username,
                _ticket.DisplayName,
                _ticket.PasswordHash,
                _ticket.TotpSecretBase32,
                passkey),
            HttpContext.RequestAborted);

        // Whatever happens next, the wizard is finished with the cookie.
        SetupCookie.Delete(HttpContext.Response);

        if (result.Status == FirstAdministratorBootstrapStatus.AdministratorAlreadyExists)
        {
            // Lost the race (or setup already ran): behave as if /setup is gone.
            _adminExists = true;
            _ticket = null;
            HttpContext.Response.StatusCode = StatusCodes.Status404NotFound;
            return;
        }

        // Created: sign the new administrator in on this response, then show the codes once.
        Person? administrator = await UserManager.FindByIdAsync(_ticket.PersonIdentifier.ToString());
        if (administrator is not null)
        {
            await SignInManager.SignInAsync(administrator, isPersistent: true);
        }

        _completedUsername = _ticket.Username;
        _recoveryCodes = result.RecoveryCodes;
        _completed = true;
        _ticket = null;
    }

    private void RestartAsync()
    {
        SetupCookie.Delete(HttpContext.Response);
        Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.Setup);
    }

    // Writes the protected ticket cookie and follows post/redirect/get so a refresh does not re-post
    // the step. NavigateTo during static SSR surfaces as an HTTP redirect carrying the fresh cookie.
    private void WriteTicketAndReload(SetupTicket ticket)
    {
        SetupCookie.Write(HttpContext.Response, _protector.Protect(ticket));
        Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.Setup);
    }

    private void PrepareTotpDisplay(SetupTicket ticket)
    {
        string provisioningUri = TotpProvisioningUri.Build(Options.RestaurantName, ticket.Username, ticket.TotpSecretBase32!);
        _qrCodeSvg = TotpQrCode.RenderSvg(provisioningUri);
        _manualEntrySecret = GroupForManualEntry(ticket.TotpSecretBase32!);
    }

    // The active step index (Account=0, Passkey=1, Authenticator=2, Finish=3).
    private int ActiveStepIndex => _ticket is null
        ? 0
        : _ticket.Step switch
        {
            SetupStep.Passkey => 1,
            SetupStep.Totp => 2,
            _ => 3,
        };

    private string StepClass(int index)
        => index == ActiveStepIndex ? "setup-step is-current"
            : index < ActiveStepIndex ? "setup-step is-done"
            : "setup-step";

    /// <summary>Groups the Base32 secret into space-separated blocks of four for easier manual entry.</summary>
    private static string GroupForManualEntry(string base32)
    {
        StringBuilder builder = new(base32.Length + (base32.Length / 4));
        for (int index = 0; index < base32.Length; index++)
        {
            if (index > 0 && index % 4 == 0)
            {
                builder.Append(' ');
            }

            builder.Append(base32[index]);
        }

        return builder.ToString();
    }

    private sealed class AccountInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Choose a username.")]
        [StringLength(64, MinimumLength = 3, ErrorMessage = "The username must be 3–64 characters.")]
        public string Username { get; set; } = string.Empty;

        [StringLength(120, ErrorMessage = "That display name is too long.")]
        public string? DisplayName { get; set; }

        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Choose a password.")]
        [StringLength(256, MinimumLength = 12, ErrorMessage = "Use at least 12 characters.")]
        public string Password { get; set; } = string.Empty;

        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Re-enter the password.")]
        [Compare(nameof(Password), ErrorMessage = "The passwords do not match.")]
        public string ConfirmPassword { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }

    private sealed class TotpInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter the six-digit code from your authenticator app.")]
        public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}

@page "/table"
@attribute [Authorize(Policy = AuthorizationPolicies.Table)]

@* The first [Authorize]-gated area (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.7 — `area.table`: any authenticated
   person). This interactive placeholder exists to prove the whole chain live — cookie → claims →
   policy → AuthorizeRouteView → circuit — before the real guest experience (joining, staging,
   sending; M3/M4) replaces its body. Anonymous visitors are redirected to /sign-in with this page
   as the return URL. *@

<PageTitle>Your table</PageTitle>

<section class="panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">Table area</p>
    <h1>Signed in and gated</h1>

    <AuthorizeView>
        <Authorized>
            <p class="lede">
                You are signed in as <strong>@context.User.Identity?.Name</strong>@DisplayNameSuffix(context.User),
                and the <code>area.table</code> policy admitted you — the authentication cookie, the
                claims principal, and the area authorization are all live.
            </p>
        </Authorized>
    </AuthorizeView>

    <p class="lede">
        Joining a table by scanning its rotating code, and building and sending orders, arrive with
        Milestones&nbsp;3 and&nbsp;4.
    </p>

    <p><a href="/">Back to the home page</a></p>
</section>

@code {
    private static string DisplayNameSuffix(System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal user)
    {
        string? displayName = user.FindFirst(RestaurantClaimTypes.DisplayName)?.Value;
        return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(displayName) ? string.Empty : $" ({displayName})";
    }
}

@* Top-level router. AuthorizeRouteView enforces [Authorize] on interactive pages: an anonymous
   visitor is sent to the sign-in page with the destination preserved, while an authenticated
   principal that fails an area policy (§3.7) sees the denial where they stand. The cascading
   authentication state comes from AddCascadingAuthenticationState (wired in AddRestaurantIdentity),
   so this works in both static SSR and interactive-server rendering. Static-SSR pages carrying
   [Authorize] are additionally enforced by endpoint authorization on the server. *@
<Router AppAssembly="typeof(Program).Assembly">
    <Found Context="routeData">
        <AuthorizeRouteView RouteData="routeData" DefaultLayout="typeof(MainLayout)">
            <NotAuthorized>
                @if (context.User.Identity?.IsAuthenticated == true)
                {
                    <section class="panel">
                        <p class="eyebrow">Access denied</p>
                        <h1>You do not have access to this area</h1>
                        <p class="lede">
                            You are signed in as <strong>@context.User.Identity?.Name</strong>, but this
                            area requires a role your account does not hold.
                        </p>
                        <p><a href="/">Back to the home page</a></p>
                    </section>
                }
                else
                {
                    <RedirectToSignIn />
                }
            </NotAuthorized>
        </AuthorizeRouteView>
        <FocusOnNavigate RouteData="routeData" Selector="h1" />
    </Found>
</Router>

using System.Globalization;

namespace MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Configuration;

/// <summary>
/// The complete environment-only configuration (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §13; REQUIREMENTS §8:
/// "environment variables only"). Bound once at startup from the flat, long-named,
/// prefixed variables, then <see cref="Validate"/> is run before HTTP is bound so the process
/// fails fast on invalid security-relevant configuration (§13, §3.2 floor guard).
/// </summary>
public sealed class RestaurantOptions
{
    // Argon2 floor guard (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.2). Below any of these the process must not start.
    public const int MinimumArgon2MemoryKibibytes = 19456;
    public const int MinimumArgon2Iterations = 2;
    public const int MinimumArgon2Parallelism = 1;

    // Token/grant/pairing lower bounds (§13: "rotation/grant/pairing values ≥ 10 s / ≥ 1 min / ≥ 1 min").
    public const int MinimumTableJoinTokenRotationSeconds = 10;
    public const int MinimumTableJoinGrantMinutes = 1;
    public const int MinimumTableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes = 1;

    /// <summary>
    /// The default trusted WebAuthn origin patterns (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.3, ADR-0005). Cloudflare
    /// Quick Tunnels hand out a random <c>*.trycloudflare.com</c> hostname per run, so trusting that
    /// wildcard lets passkeys work in a quick-tunnel demo without knowing the URL at startup. This gates
    /// only WebAuthn relying-party derivation; it is deliberately not a general CORS allowance.
    /// </summary>
    public static readonly IReadOnlyList<string> DefaultTrustedOriginPatterns = ["https://*.trycloudflare.com"];

    public required string RestaurantName { get; init; }
    public required string PublicOrigin { get; init; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Additional browser origins that may act as the WebAuthn relying party, as wildcard patterns
    /// (<c>RESTAURANT_TRUSTED_ORIGIN_PATTERNS</c>; §3.3, ADR-0005). The configured <see cref="PublicOrigin"/>
    /// and loopback (in development) are always trusted; these extend that set so a Cloudflare quick
    /// tunnel's per-run <c>*.trycloudflare.com</c> host works without being known at startup. Defaults to
    /// <see cref="DefaultTrustedOriginPatterns"/>; not required so existing constructions keep the default.
    /// </summary>
    public IReadOnlyList<string> TrustedOriginPatterns { get; init; } = DefaultTrustedOriginPatterns;

    public required string TimeZoneId { get; init; }
    public required string CurrencyCode { get; init; }
    public required string DatabaseConnectionString { get; init; }
    public required string DataProtectionKeysDirectory { get; init; }
    public required int KitchenSubmissionReminderSeconds { get; init; }
    public required int TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds { get; init; }
    public required int TableJoinGrantMinutes { get; init; }
    public required int TableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes { get; init; }
    public required int Argon2MemoryKibibytes { get; init; }
    public required int Argon2Iterations { get; init; }
    public required int Argon2Parallelism { get; init; }
    public required int Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes { get; init; }

    public static RestaurantOptions FromConfiguration(IConfiguration configuration)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(configuration);

        return new RestaurantOptions
        {
            RestaurantName = ReadString(configuration, "RESTAURANT_NAME", "My Restaurant"),
            PublicOrigin = ReadString(configuration, "RESTAURANT_PUBLIC_ORIGIN", "https://localhost:8443"),
            TrustedOriginPatterns = ReadOriginPatterns(configuration, "RESTAURANT_TRUSTED_ORIGIN_PATTERNS", DefaultTrustedOriginPatterns),
            TimeZoneId = ReadString(configuration, "RESTAURANT_TIME_ZONE", "America/New_York"),
            CurrencyCode = ReadString(configuration, "RESTAURANT_CURRENCY_CODE", "USD"),
            DatabaseConnectionString = ReadString(
                configuration,
                "RESTAURANT_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING",
                "Host=localhost;Port=5432;Database=myrestaurant;Username=myrestaurant;Password=myrestaurant"),
            DataProtectionKeysDirectory = ReadString(configuration, "DATA_PROTECTION_KEYS_DIRECTORY", "/var/lib/myrestaurant/dataprotection"),
            KitchenSubmissionReminderSeconds = ReadInt(configuration, "KITCHEN_SUBMISSION_REMINDER_SECONDS", 60),
            TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds = ReadInt(configuration, "TABLE_JOIN_TOKEN_ROTATION_SECONDS", 60),
            TableJoinGrantMinutes = ReadInt(configuration, "TABLE_JOIN_GRANT_MINUTES", 10),
            TableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes = ReadInt(configuration, "TABLE_DISPLAY_PAIRING_CODE_MINUTES", 10),
            Argon2MemoryKibibytes = ReadInt(configuration, "ARGON2_MEMORY_KIBIBYTES", 65536),
            Argon2Iterations = ReadInt(configuration, "ARGON2_ITERATIONS", 3),
            Argon2Parallelism = ReadInt(configuration, "ARGON2_PARALLELISM", 1),
            Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes = ReadInt(configuration, "ARGON2_MAX_CONCURRENT_HASHES", 4),
        };
    }

    /// <summary>Returns a human-readable reason for every invalid setting; empty means valid.</summary>
    public IReadOnlyList<string> Validate()
    {
        List<string> errors = [];

        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(DatabaseConnectionString))
        {
            errors.Add("RESTAURANT_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING must be set.");
        }

        if (!Uri.TryCreate(PublicOrigin, UriKind.Absolute, out Uri? origin)
            || !string.Equals(origin.Scheme, Uri.UriSchemeHttps, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        {
            errors.Add($"RESTAURANT_PUBLIC_ORIGIN must be an absolute https URL (was '{PublicOrigin}').");
        }

        if (!TryResolveTimeZone(TimeZoneId))
        {
            errors.Add($"RESTAURANT_TIME_ZONE '{TimeZoneId}' is not a resolvable time zone on this host.");
        }

        if (CurrencyCode.Length != 3 || !CurrencyCode.All(char.IsAsciiLetter))
        {
            errors.Add($"RESTAURANT_CURRENCY_CODE must be a 3-letter ISO 4217 code (was '{CurrencyCode}').");
        }

        if (Argon2MemoryKibibytes < MinimumArgon2MemoryKibibytes)
        {
            errors.Add($"ARGON2_MEMORY_KIBIBYTES must be at least {MinimumArgon2MemoryKibibytes} (was {Argon2MemoryKibibytes}).");
        }

        if (Argon2Iterations < MinimumArgon2Iterations)
        {
            errors.Add($"ARGON2_ITERATIONS must be at least {MinimumArgon2Iterations} (was {Argon2Iterations}).");
        }

        if (Argon2Parallelism < MinimumArgon2Parallelism)
        {
            errors.Add($"ARGON2_PARALLELISM must be at least {MinimumArgon2Parallelism} (was {Argon2Parallelism}).");
        }

        if (Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes < 1)
        {
            errors.Add($"ARGON2_MAX_CONCURRENT_HASHES must be at least 1 (was {Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes}).");
        }

        if (TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds < MinimumTableJoinTokenRotationSeconds)
        {
            errors.Add($"TABLE_JOIN_TOKEN_ROTATION_SECONDS must be at least {MinimumTableJoinTokenRotationSeconds} (was {TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds}).");
        }

        if (TableJoinGrantMinutes < MinimumTableJoinGrantMinutes)
        {
            errors.Add($"TABLE_JOIN_GRANT_MINUTES must be at least {MinimumTableJoinGrantMinutes} (was {TableJoinGrantMinutes}).");
        }

        if (TableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes < MinimumTableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes)
        {
            errors.Add($"TABLE_DISPLAY_PAIRING_CODE_MINUTES must be at least {MinimumTableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes} (was {TableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes}).");
        }

        if (KitchenSubmissionReminderSeconds < 1)
        {
            errors.Add($"KITCHEN_SUBMISSION_REMINDER_SECONDS must be at least 1 (was {KitchenSubmissionReminderSeconds}).");
        }

        foreach (string pattern in TrustedOriginPatterns)
        {
            if (!IsValidOriginPattern(pattern))
            {
                errors.Add($"RESTAURANT_TRUSTED_ORIGIN_PATTERNS entry '{pattern}' must be an https origin like 'https://*.trycloudflare.com' (scheme://host, optional leading '*.' wildcard label, no path or port).");
            }
        }

        return errors;
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// The host of the configured public origin (§3.3). As of ADR-0005 the WebAuthn relying-party ID
    /// is derived <em>per request</em> from the request host (so a quick tunnel's per-run hostname
    /// works), not pinned to this value; this remains the canonical configured host — used for QR join
    /// URLs (ADR-0009) and as the fallback host presented by <see cref="Identity.PublicOriginMiddleware"/>.
    /// </summary>
    public string ResolveWebAuthnRelyingPartyId() => new Uri(PublicOrigin).Host;

    /// <summary>The configured display time zone (validated at startup).</summary>
    public TimeZoneInfo ResolveTimeZone() => TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById(TimeZoneId);

    private static bool TryResolveTimeZone(string timeZoneId)
    {
        try
        {
            _ = TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById(timeZoneId);
            return true;
        }
        catch (Exception exception) when (exception is TimeZoneNotFoundException or InvalidTimeZoneException)
        {
            return false;
        }
    }

    private static string ReadString(IConfiguration configuration, string key, string fallback)
    {
        string? value = configuration[key];
        return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) ? fallback : value;
    }

    private static int ReadInt(IConfiguration configuration, string key, int fallback)
        => int.TryParse(configuration[key], NumberStyles.Integer, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out int value)
            ? value
            : fallback;

    /// <summary>
    /// Reads a comma/space/newline-separated list of origin patterns, trimming empties; returns the
    /// supplied default when the variable is unset or contains no non-empty entries.
    /// </summary>
    private static IReadOnlyList<string> ReadOriginPatterns(
        IConfiguration configuration,
        string key,
        IReadOnlyList<string> fallback)
    {
        string? raw = configuration[key];
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(raw))
        {
            return fallback;
        }

        char[] separators = new[] { ',', ' ', '\t', '\n', '\r' };
        string[] parts = raw.Split(separators, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries | StringSplitOptions.TrimEntries);
        return parts.Length == 0 ? fallback : parts;
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// A trusted-origin pattern is <c>scheme://host</c> with an https scheme, a non-empty host that
    /// may begin with a single <c>*.</c> wildcard label, and no path, query, fragment, userinfo, or
    /// port. This matches the runtime matcher in <see cref="Identity.WebAuthnOriginPolicy"/>.
    /// </summary>
    private static bool IsValidOriginPattern(string pattern)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pattern))
        {
            return false;
        }

        string value = pattern.Trim().ToLowerInvariant();
        const string prefix = "https://";
        if (!value.StartsWith(prefix, StringComparison.Ordinal))
        {
            return false;
        }

        string host = value[prefix.Length..];
        if (host.Length == 0 || host.AsSpan().ContainsAny("/?#@ :"))
        {
            return false;
        }

        string bare = host.StartsWith("*.", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? host[2..] : host;
        return bare.Length > 0 && !bare.Contains('*') && bare.Contains('.');
    }
}

using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Antiforgery;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;
using MyRestaurant.DataAccess.Identity;
using MyRestaurant.Domain.Time;

namespace MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Identity;

/// <summary>
/// The non-page account endpoints. Sign-out is a POST endpoint rather than a page
/// (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.5): it must clear the authentication cookies on the response, must
/// never be triggerable by a crafted GET link, and must stay reachable while the obligations
/// pipeline blocks everything else (its path is exempt in <see cref="ObligationsEnforcement"/>).
/// Binding the optional form field makes the endpoint "accept form data", which switches on the
/// framework's automatic antiforgery validation for it — the layout's sign-out form supplies the
/// token via <c>&lt;AntiforgeryToken /&gt;</c>.
///
/// <para>The two passkey-options endpoints (§3.3) return the WebAuthn ceremony JSON that the browser
/// feeds to <c>navigator.credentials</c>. They are fetched by the <c>passkey-submit</c> element, not
/// posted by a form, so they validate the antiforgery token manually from the request header (the
/// element carries it) rather than via form binding. Creation options require an authenticated user
/// (you register a passkey for yourself); request options are anonymous, because sign-in — including
/// the username-first and discoverable flows — happens before there is a session.</para>
/// </summary>
public static class AccountEndpoints
{
    public static IEndpointRouteBuilder MapRestaurantAccountEndpoints(this IEndpointRouteBuilder endpoints)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(endpoints);

        endpoints.MapPost(AccountRoutes.SignOut, async (
            SignInManager<Person> signInManager,
            [FromForm] string? returnUrl) =>
        {
            // Clears the application, external, and both two-factor cookies. Harmless when the
            // caller was already anonymous. No security_event is written: sign-out is not in the
            // §8.2 event-type vocabulary (sessions also end silently by expiry and stamp rotation,
            // so recording only explicit sign-outs would tell a misleading story).
            await signInManager.SignOutAsync();
            return Results.LocalRedirect(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(returnUrl));
        });

        // Registration ceremony options for the signed-in user (attestation). The user entity's Id is
        // the WebAuthn user handle; the framework stashes the challenge in a short-lived cookie that
        // PerformPasskeyAttestationAsync later reads.
        endpoints.MapPost(AccountRoutes.PasskeyCreationOptions, async (
            HttpContext context,
            UserManager<Person> userManager,
            SignInManager<Person> signInManager,
            IAntiforgery antiforgery) =>
        {
            await antiforgery.ValidateRequestAsync(context);

            Person? user = await userManager.GetUserAsync(context.User);
            if (user is null)
            {
                return Results.Unauthorized();
            }

            string userId = await userManager.GetUserIdAsync(user);
            string userName = await userManager.GetUserNameAsync(user) ?? user.Username;
            string optionsJson = await signInManager.MakePasskeyCreationOptionsAsync(new PasskeyUserEntity
            {
                Id = userId,
                Name = userName,
                DisplayName = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(user.DisplayName) ? userName : user.DisplayName,
            });

            return Results.Content(optionsJson, "application/json");
        }).RequireAuthorization();

        // Assertion ceremony options for sign-in (anonymous). With a username we scope allowCredentials
        // to that account (username-first); without one we return no allowCredentials so the browser
        // offers any discoverable passkey for this relying party.
        endpoints.MapPost(AccountRoutes.PasskeyRequestOptions, async (
            HttpContext context,
            UserManager<Person> userManager,
            SignInManager<Person> signInManager,
            IAntiforgery antiforgery,
            [FromQuery] string? username) =>
        {
            await antiforgery.ValidateRequestAsync(context);

            Person? user = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(username)
                ? null
                : await userManager.FindByNameAsync(username);
            string optionsJson = await signInManager.MakePasskeyRequestOptionsAsync(user);

            return Results.Content(optionsJson, "application/json");
        });

        // Registration ceremony options for the FIRST-ADMINISTRATOR wizard (§3.6). Anonymous — there is
        // no session during setup — and gated on the zero-administrator condition, so it is dead the
        // moment an administrator exists. The pending identity lives in the setup cookie (the wizard
        // minted the person id at its first step); we use that id as the WebAuthn user handle so it
        // equals the account the wizard will create, which a later discoverable sign-in relies on. Like
        // the other options endpoints it is fetched (not form-posted), so the antiforgery token is read
        // from the request header the passkey element supplies.
        endpoints.MapPost(AccountRoutes.SetupPasskeyCreationOptions, async (
            HttpContext context,
            IFirstAdministratorBootstrap bootstrap,
            SignInManager<Person> signInManager,
            IAntiforgery antiforgery,
            IDataProtectionProvider dataProtectionProvider,
            IClock clock) =>
        {
            await antiforgery.ValidateRequestAsync(context);

            if (await bootstrap.AdministratorExistsAsync(context.RequestAborted))
            {
                return Results.NotFound();
            }

            SetupTicketProtector protector = new(dataProtectionProvider);
            if (!context.Request.Cookies.TryGetValue(SetupCookie.Name, out string? cookie)
                || !protector.TryUnprotect(cookie, out SetupTicket? ticket)
                || ticket is null
                || ticket.HasExpired(clock.UtcNow, SetupCookie.Lifetime))
            {
                // No valid in-flight setup — the wizard must be (re)started before a passkey can be added.
                return Results.BadRequest();
            }

            string optionsJson = await signInManager.MakePasskeyCreationOptionsAsync(new PasskeyUserEntity
            {
                Id = ticket.PersonIdentifier.ToString(),
                Name = ticket.Username,
                DisplayName = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ticket.DisplayName) ? ticket.Username : ticket.DisplayName,
            });

            return Results.Content(optionsJson, "application/json");
        });

        return endpoints;
    }
}

using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.Cookies;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Extensions;
using MyRestaurant.DataAccess;
using MyRestaurant.DataAccess.Identity;
using MyRestaurant.Domain.Identifiers;
using MyRestaurant.Domain.Time;
using MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Authorization;
using MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Configuration;
using MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Observability;

namespace MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Identity;

/// <summary>
/// Wires ASP.NET Core Identity <b>core</b> services (never the EF default stores/UI) over the custom
/// Dapper store, with the Argon2id hasher replacing Identity's PBKDF2 default
/// (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.1–§3.2, ADR-0003/ADR-0008), plus sign-in and authorization:
/// <list type="bullet">
///   <item>the Identity cookie scheme, hardened (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, 24-hour sliding),
///   pointing at the sign-in / sign-out / access-denied surfaces in <see cref="AccountRoutes"/>;</item>
///   <item><see cref="RestaurantSignInManager"/> (the auditing <see cref="SignInManager{TUser}"/>,
///   which also refuses deactivated accounts);</item>
///   <item><see cref="RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory"/> — emits the role claims the area policies
///   match on (the single-generic default factory never does), plus the §3.5 obligation claims and
///   the display name;</item>
///   <item>security-stamp revalidation every 5 minutes, so resets, role revocations, and
///   deactivations bite live sessions within minutes (§3.1);</item>
///   <item>the area authorization policies (§3.7), via <see cref="AuthorizationServiceCollectionExtensions"/>;</item>
///   <item>the cascading <c>Task&lt;AuthenticationState&gt;</c> the Blazor router and
///   <c>AuthorizeView</c> consume;</item>
///   <item>the append-only <see cref="ISecurityEventLog"/> that sign-in outcomes are recorded to (§3.5);</item>
///   <item>the read-only <see cref="IPersonDirectory"/> the administration people list reads from (§3.6/§3.7);</item>
///   <item>the transactional <see cref="IAccountAdministration"/> the administration tools write through
///   (create staff, grant/revoke roles, reset credentials, deactivate/reactivate — §3.7).</item>
/// </list>
///
/// The obligations pipeline itself is enforced by <see cref="ObligationsMiddleware"/> in the request
/// pipeline (registered in <c>Program.cs</c>); the sign-in and forced-change pages are static-SSR
/// Razor components under <c>Components/Account</c>.
/// </summary>
public static class IdentityServiceCollectionExtensions
{
    /// <summary>The application authentication cookie name.</summary>
    private const string AuthenticationCookieName = "myrestaurant.authentication";

    /// <summary>Security-stamp revalidation interval (§3.1).</summary>
    private static readonly TimeSpan SecurityStampValidationInterval = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(5);

    /// <summary>Cookie sliding-expiration lifetime (§3.1).</summary>
    private static readonly TimeSpan AuthenticationCookieLifetime = TimeSpan.FromHours(24);

    public static IServiceCollection AddRestaurantIdentity(this IServiceCollection services, RestaurantOptions options)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(services);
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(options);

        // SignInManager and the cookie handlers resolve the current HttpContext.
        services.AddHttpContextAccessor();

        // Cookie authentication with Identity's four schemes (application, external, two-factor
        // remember-me, two-factor user-id). The application cookie is the default for
        // authenticate/challenge/forbid; the external cookie is the default sign-in scheme, matching
        // what AddIdentity configures internally (we compose it by hand to avoid a RoleManager).
        services.AddAuthentication(authentication =>
            {
                authentication.DefaultAuthenticateScheme = IdentityConstants.ApplicationScheme;
                authentication.DefaultChallengeScheme = IdentityConstants.ApplicationScheme;
                authentication.DefaultSignInScheme = IdentityConstants.ExternalScheme;
            })
            .AddIdentityCookies();

        // Area authorization policies (§3.7).
        services.AddRestaurantAuthorization();

        // The cascading Task<AuthenticationState> that <AuthorizeView>, AuthorizeRouteView, and the
        // account pages consume — in both static SSR and interactive-server rendering.
        services.AddCascadingAuthenticationState();

        services.AddIdentityCore<Person>(identity =>
            {
                // Usernames: 3–64 chars is enforced by the DB CHECK and citext handles uniqueness, so
                // do not additionally restrict the character set (empty = "allow any"); email is optional.
                identity.User.AllowedUserNameCharacters = string.Empty;
                identity.User.RequireUniqueEmail = false;

                // Password policy (§3.2): length 12, no composition rules, no expiry.
                identity.Password.RequiredLength = 12;
                identity.Password.RequiredUniqueChars = 1;
                identity.Password.RequireDigit = false;
                identity.Password.RequireLowercase = false;
                identity.Password.RequireUppercase = false;
                identity.Password.RequireNonAlphanumeric = false;

                // Lockout (§3.1): 5 consecutive failures lock for 5 minutes, applied to everyone.
                identity.Lockout.AllowedForNewUsers = true;
                identity.Lockout.MaxFailedAccessAttempts = 5;
                identity.Lockout.DefaultLockoutTimeSpan = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(5);

                // No email/phone confirmation gating (optional contact fields only, §11.1).
                identity.SignIn.RequireConfirmedAccount = false;
                identity.SignIn.RequireConfirmedEmail = false;
                identity.SignIn.RequireConfirmedPhoneNumber = false;

                // No personal-data protection layer over the store (we manage TOTP encryption ourselves).
                identity.Stores.ProtectPersonalData = false;
            })
            .AddUserStore<DapperUserStore>()
            .AddClaimsPrincipalFactory<RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory>()
            .AddDefaultTokenProviders() // Authenticator (TOTP), Data-Protection, email/phone token providers.
            // Override the built-in authenticator provider (±2-step window) with the §3.4 ±1 one.
            // Identity's provider map keeps the last registration under a given name, so registering
            // ours under the same DefaultAuthenticatorProvider name after the defaults wins.
            .AddTokenProvider<RestaurantAuthenticatorTokenProvider>(TokenOptions.DefaultAuthenticatorProvider)
            .AddSignInManager<RestaurantSignInManager>();

        // AddIdentityCookies wires the application cookie's OnValidatePrincipal to the static
        // SecurityStampValidator, which resolves these at runtime. AddIdentityCore/AddSignInManager do
        // not register them (only the monolithic AddIdentity does), so register them explicitly.
        services.TryAddScoped<ISecurityStampValidator, SecurityStampValidator<Person>>();
        services.TryAddScoped<ITwoFactorSecurityStampValidator, TwoFactorSecurityStampValidator<Person>>();

        // The .NET 10 WebAuthn ceremony handler (§3.3). Same story as the stamp validators: the
        // monolithic AddIdentity registers it, AddIdentityCore does not (verified against the framework
        // source), so register it explicitly or MakePasskey*OptionsAsync throws "requires an
        // IPasskeyHandler service". It reads the options configured just below.
        services.TryAddScoped<IPasskeyHandler<Person>, PasskeyHandler<Person>>();

        // The WebAuthn origin-trust policy (§3.3, ADR-0005): which browser origins may act as the
        // relying party (the configured origin + trusted wildcard patterns + loopback in dev) and what
        // host the app presents so the RP ID derives correctly. Shared by ValidateOrigin below and by
        // PublicOriginMiddleware. Singleton — it is immutable configuration.
        WebAuthnOriginPolicy originPolicy = new(options.PublicOrigin, options.TrustedOriginPatterns);
        services.AddSingleton(originPolicy);

        // Relying-party options for every passkey ceremony (§3.3). ServerDomain is left NULL on
        // purpose (ADR-0005 course correction): the .NET 10 handler then derives the RP ID from the
        // request host (options.ServerDomain ?? Request.Host.Host), which PublicOriginMiddleware has
        // normalized to the browser's real origin host. Pinning it to a boot-time value is exactly what
        // made passkeys impossible behind a Cloudflare quick tunnel (its *.trycloudflare.com hostname
        // is random per run and unknown at startup); deriving per request makes them self-healing. This
        // is safe because credentials are RP-ID-scoped by the authenticator, and ValidateOrigin gates
        // the browser's signed origin against the trusted set as defence in depth. residentKey and
        // userVerification are "preferred" (discoverable + username-first both work; verification is
        // encouraged, not demanded); attestation is left at the browser default of "none".
        services.Configure<IdentityPasskeyOptions>(passkey =>
        {
            passkey.ServerDomain = null;
            passkey.UserVerificationRequirement = "preferred";
            passkey.ResidentKeyRequirement = "preferred";
            passkey.ValidateOrigin = context =>
                ValueTask.FromResult(!context.CrossOrigin && originPolicy.IsTrustedOrigin(context.Origin));
        });

        // Harden the application cookie (§3.1). Secure + HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax; 24-hour sliding
        // expiration. The login/logout/access-denied paths are the real account surfaces now (this
        // slice); an unauthenticated hit on an [Authorize] page redirects to /sign-in?ReturnUrl=….
        services.ConfigureApplicationCookie(cookie =>
        {
            cookie.Cookie.Name = AuthenticationCookieName;
            cookie.Cookie.HttpOnly = true;
            cookie.Cookie.SecurePolicy = CookieSecurePolicy.Always;
            cookie.Cookie.SameSite = SameSiteMode.Lax;
            cookie.ExpireTimeSpan = AuthenticationCookieLifetime;
            cookie.SlidingExpiration = true;
            cookie.LoginPath = AccountRoutes.SignIn;
            cookie.LogoutPath = AccountRoutes.SignOut;
            cookie.AccessDeniedPath = AccountRoutes.AccessDenied;
        });

        // Revalidate the security stamp every 5 minutes so administrative resets/revocations/
        // deactivations invalidate live sessions promptly (§3.1). Rebuilding the principal also
        // refreshes the role and obligation claims through the factory above.
        services.Configure<SecurityStampValidatorOptions>(validator =>
        {
            validator.ValidationInterval = SecurityStampValidationInterval;
        });

        // Replace Identity's PBKDF2 IPasswordHasher<Person> with Argon2id (§3.2). Singleton so the
        // process-wide concurrency semaphore genuinely bounds total concurrent hashes; the duration
        // hook feeds password_hash_duration_milliseconds (§12) without DataAccess knowing about metrics.
        services.Replace(ServiceDescriptor.Singleton<IPasswordHasher<Person>>(serviceProvider =>
        {
            RestaurantMetrics metrics = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<RestaurantMetrics>();
            return new Argon2idPasswordHasher(
                new Argon2HashingOptions(
                    options.Argon2MemoryKibibytes,
                    options.Argon2Iterations,
                    options.Argon2Parallelism,
                    options.Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes),
                metrics.RecordPasswordHashDuration);
        }));

        // The append-only security-event trail (§3.5, §3.7). Scoped, matching the Identity lifetime;
        // it holds no state and opens a connection per write from the singleton factory.
        services.AddScoped<ISecurityEventLog, DapperSecurityEventLog>();

        // Read-only people directory for the administration area (§3.6/§3.7). Scoped, matching the
        // Identity lifetime; it holds no state and opens a connection per read from the singleton
        // factory. Its only dependency is IDatabaseConnectionFactory, so a plain type registration
        // resolves it.
        services.AddScoped<IPersonDirectory, DapperPersonDirectory>();

        // TOTP enrollment (§3.4) for the voluntary and forced pages. Scoped so it shares the request's
        // UserManager/DapperUserStore instance (it mutates the tracked entity through the store cast);
        // the factory closes over the configured RESTAURANT_NAME, which is the provisioning issuer (§13).
        services.AddScoped(serviceProvider => new TotpEnrollment(
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<UserManager<Person>>(),
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IUserStore<Person>>(),
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<ISecurityEventLog>(),
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IDataProtectionProvider>(),
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IClock>(),
            options.RestaurantName));

        // First-administrator bootstrap (§3.6). Writes the whole first account — person, passkey, TOTP
        // secret, recovery codes, and the self-granted administrator role — in one advisory-locked
        // transaction, and answers the zero-administrator gate the /setup page and endpoint consult.
        // Scoped so it shares the request's connection-factory and data-protection lifetimes; it holds
        // no state and opens its own connection/transaction per commit from the singleton factory.
        services.AddScoped<IFirstAdministratorBootstrap>(serviceProvider => new DapperFirstAdministratorBootstrap(
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IDatabaseConnectionFactory>(),
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IClock>(),
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IIdentifierFactory>(),
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IDataProtectionProvider>()));

        // Account administration (§3.7): create staff, grant/revoke roles (with the acting administrator
        // recorded as grantor), reset credentials, and deactivate/reactivate. Each operation is one
        // transaction that also rotates the subject's security stamp (§3.1) and records the matching
        // security_event. Scoped like the bootstrap it reuses; it holds no state and opens its own
        // connection/transaction per operation from the singleton factory. No data-protection dependency
        // — it only clears TOTP secrets, never writes one.
        services.AddScoped<IAccountAdministration>(serviceProvider => new DapperAccountAdministration(
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IDatabaseConnectionFactory>(),
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IClock>(),
            serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IIdentifierFactory>()));

        return services;
    }
}
